language.string: Boats and warships gather food and earn money faster. + Ushkuinik creation limit
Powerful heavy musketeer who makes unpopular decisions.
Ataman of the Vyatkin Brigade, making unpopular decisions. After defeat, the ataman can be rehired in the tavern.
Brigadiers gain increased attack strength and health.
Brigadiers gain significantly increased attack strength and health.

Ataman Ivan Vyatich arrives on the battlefield with a small regiment of brigadiers. Vyatich strengthens nearby Vyatkin brigade regiments and can replenish personnel. If defeated, Vyatich goes to drown his sorrows in the tavern, from where he can return to the battlefield for a hefty sum.
Sends 3 Cossack-bandits. Allows the use of Cossack-bandits in taverns and stables for fewer population slots. All bandits gain increased health.

Supplies a rickshaw for the dock and allows the production of fire ships at the dock. Explosive ships are filled with incendiary mixture, contact with which deals significant damage. + Ushkuinik creation limit
River pirate dealing area damage with axes, effective against heavy infantry. Can build ports and factories. Restores health when idle.
Ushkuiniks gain increased attack strength and health, as well as a higher creation limit.

Sign a fake alliance with river pirates. Allows training of ushkuiniks (infantry units similar to urumi) in local embassies and upgrading them. Also grants access to technologies aimed at improving bandits and fishing, each technology increases the Ushkuinik creation limit.
Allows training of Bashkir archers in local embassies and upgrading them. Bashkir archers now occupy population slots.
Increases the melee attack strength of bandits, doubly so for Ushkuiniks. Ushkuiniks gain access to a reloadable axe-throwing attack. + Ushkuinik creation limit
Call local rogues to aid your colonies. + Ushkuinik creation limit
Heavily armed guard in the service of a banking family. Effective against bandits, mercenaries, and heroes.
Allows building capybaras in pens, and capybaras periodically appear from pens. When gathering resources from capybaras, more experience is gained instead of food.
First wave of feminism — supplies 20 feminist-frakocrats and allows reviving them in barracks with the start of the industrial age.
Loads rifles with grapeshot, increasing firing rate and area of effect but reducing aimed range.
Delivers 3 grenadiers to you and your team. Your allies can train basic grenadiers in barracks.
Yermak Timofeyevich's expedition will help you form alliances with local settlements.
Cavalry and assault infantry move faster, their melee attacks deal area damage, especially in trample mode. All melee damage dealt by cavalry is reduced.
All German settlers and pikemen become assault grenadiers, and settler wagons and houses are armed with zweihänders. Sets a population limit of 100, independent of houses.
Light assault siege infantry using grenades against cavalry and buildings.
Allows hiring grenadiers in the artillery foundry. Supplies grenadiers and doppelsoldners with mortars. Also supplies several grenadiers, +3 grenadiers.
Reduces the cost of hiring all light cavalry.
Fast heavy infantry, effective against cavalry. Armed with a single-shot pistol to fight lightly armed mounted shooters at a distance.
Sends Turkish swordsmen (silahdars), now each shipment includes an additional Silahdar.
Increases the attack strength of silahdars, allows your swordsmen to gain combat promotions, additionally increasing movement speed and attack speed for each enemy military unit destroyed.
Increases the health of silahdars, allows your swordsmen to deal area damage in melee, reducing effectiveness against light infantry.
Converts all your town centers into fort wagons. Increases fort construction limit by +3. Allows building settlers in forts. Town centers are cheaper but take longer to build.

Revolt! All trained units take up European weapons:
 • Peasants turn into revolutionaries
 • Tomahawks turn into fencibles
 • Musket riders turn into carbine riders
 • Canya riders turn into guard hussars
 • Aennas turn into Canadian lumberjacks (strelets)
 • Forest wanderers turn into snipers
 • Rams turn into guard grenadiers
 • Light cannons turn into gatlings
 • Shield bearers turn into highlanders
Silahdars gain increased attack strength and health.
Sends 22 tomahawk warriors and changes the damage type when throwing to melee.
Onondaga sends 9 tomahawks and increases their damage against cavalry when thrown.
Cayuga sends 7 musket riders and increases their attack strength, especially against artillery.
Seneca sends 9 forest wanderers and increases their health.
Tuscarora sends 8 canya riders and makes them more resistant to siege damage.
Mohawk sends a light cannon and a mortar.\n • Artillery construction speed increases.\n • Artillery requires less population.
9 Iroquois with tomahawks and 1 local scout
Destroyed enemy military units and buildings bring a small amount of money in addition to experience. The local embassy now provides passive income.

(Team) Reduces the cost and increases the training speed of bandits. 
 • Bandit: -10% cost 
 • Bandit: -15% training time 
 Unique Lakota bonus:
 • Additional cost reduction (-10%) and bandit training speed increase (-5%).
 • Increases bandit movement speed (+10%).
 • Reduces population cost for bandits.
 • Lakota infantry and cavalry now cost more (+10%).
Bandits gain increased attack strength and health. Allows building an additional local embassy to hire bandits.
Instead of wood, villagers gather export from mangrove groves.
Restores villagers' ability to gather wood from mangrove groves instead of export.
You receive a passive influx of export. Destroying enemy buildings grants export instead of experience.
Second wave of feminism — supplies feminist-frakocrats to you and your allies for every 3 lost military units (maximum 20).
Supplies 10 haramis and allows hiring them in castles and forts for export.
Versatile shooter capable of making aimed shots effective against light infantry.
Supplies a mangrove grove wagon and allows creating them in the town center.
Gives your monastery unique Indian upgrades.\n • Also allows researching these upgrades in Indian non-military wonders.
Additional Condottieri for Germans and Italians
Hussars deal increased damage to artillery.
Allows your hussars to gain combat promotions, additionally increasing health for each enemy military unit destroyed.
Light cavalry is trained faster, especially acciradors.
Acciradors gain increased health and attack strength but occupy more population.

Supplies 1 mansabdar rider with a zamburak. Riders with zamburaks mount Turkish howitzers on camels. Changes ranged damage type to siege, allows attacking buildings at long range, removes damage multipliers when shooting at artillery.
Sends 3 Indian bandits. Bandit units require fewer population slots, allows hiring Indian bandits in the monastery, +1 villager.
All cavalry moves and is trained faster thanks to training at the Spanish Riding School in Vienna.
Supplies an additional carabineer each time you send this shipment (starting from 7).
Supplies an additional fencible each time you send this shipment (starting from 10).
Supplies additional Canadian lumberjacks each time you send this shipment (starting from 17).
Heavy melee infantry with a large attack radius. When this unit is killed, the enemy receives gold instead of experience.
Melee rider with cold weapons. Effective against light infantry. When this unit is killed, the enemy receives gold instead of experience.
Skirmisher with low health but a large attack radius, equipped with a rifled barrel without needing to upgrade it. When this unit is killed, the enemy receives gold instead of experience.
Ancient heavy infantry effective against riders and buildings. When this unit is killed, the enemy receives gold instead of experience.
Heavily armed bandit rider, absorbing part of the damage dealt to nearby allied riders. Effective against any infantry. When this unit is killed, the enemy receives gold instead of experience.
Heavily armed rider trampling enemies. Restores health when idle. When this unit is killed, the enemy receives gold instead of experience.
Heavy ranged infantry with bayonets to fight against riders. When this unit is killed, the enemy receives gold instead of experience.
Light infantry with incendiary bolts dealing burning damage. Effective against heavy infantry and lightly armed riders. When this unit is killed, the enemy receives gold instead of experience.
Replaces your trained light cavalry with war wagons, replaces light cavalry in stables with war wagons.
Supplies 4 war wagons. Allows war wagons to periodically fire artillery volleys.
Supplies 3 captured mortars and allows hiring captured mortars in the embassy and engineering workshop at a reduced cost in resources and population slots.\n • Captured mortar: -1 population cost
Arrives quickly! Already created villagers are replaced with Cree hunters, replaces villagers in the town center and deck with Cree hunters. Settlers are trained faster.
Oneida sends a town center travois and 10 villagers, villagers upon creation provide much more experience.
Supplies several falconets, instead of uhlans you receive an additional culverin.
Supplies 1 royal embassy, where the Sufi mosque provides access to Qizilbash and upgrades. Sufi units acquired from the royal embassy will require population slots.
Supplies 1 guard pikeman for every 3 pikemen lost during the game. Upgrades pikemen to guards and allows researching the imperial upgrade for them. Replaces the hiring cost of pikemen with gold.
Supplies 6 caçadores, 6 dragoons, and 6 acciradors. All units receive a slight health bonus.
Yabusame archers improve their attack and allows them to attack buildings from afar with fire arrows.

Supplies a wagon for the commandery.
 • Allows hiring in the commandery a group of settler-immigrants from one of three countries to choose from.
 • Available settler immigrants: French coureurs des bois, German settler wagons, Sicilians (Berber nomads).
 • Allows training the chosen settlers in the commandery.

Supplies a wagon for the commandery.
 • Allows hiring in the commandery a knightly order of the commerce age from one of three countries to choose from.
 • Available knightly orders: English longbowmen, Spanish rodeleros, French grenadiers.
 • Allows training knights of the chosen order in the commandery.

Supplies a wagon for the commandery.
 • Allows hiring in the commandery a professional knightly order from one of three countries to choose from.
 • Available knightly orders: Spanish garrochistas, Portuguese caçadores, German doppelsoldners.
 • Allows training knights of the chosen order in the commandery.

Supplies a wagon for the commandery.
 • Allows hiring in the commandery a regiment of light cavalry from one of three countries to choose from.
 • Available regiments: Russian horse archers, Portuguese dragoons, Dutch carabineers.
 • Allows training knights of the chosen regiment in the commandery.

Supplies a wagon for the commandery.
 • Allows hiring in the commandery a knightly order of specialized units from one of three countries to choose from.
 • Available orders: French gendarmes, Russian oprichniki, Italian schiavoni.
 • Allows training knights of the chosen order in the commandery.

Commanderies can support additional population, training order units costs less. Increases attack and health of order units. 
 • Commandery: population support +10
 • Units from commandery: -15% cost
 • Units from commandery: +20% health 
 • Units from commandery: +20% unit "All Actions" damage

Sends a detachment of Magyar hussars. Allows hiring them in the commandery. \n • Choosing this technology blocks access to other unique Maltese technologies of the fifth age. \n • This upgrade becomes cheaper with accumulated experience for training units, constructing buildings, ordering shipments, researching technologies, and upgrades.

Sends a unique Italian development - a tank. Allows restoring it in the commandery. \n • Choosing this technology blocks access to other unique Maltese technologies of the fifth age. \n • This upgrade becomes cheaper with accumulated experience for training units, constructing buildings, ordering shipments, researching technologies, and upgrades.

Supplies a factory wagon from which a factory can be built. Increases the factory construction limit. \n • Choosing this technology blocks access to other unique Maltese technologies of the fifth age. \n • This upgrade becomes cheaper with accumulated experience for training units, constructing buildings, ordering shipments, researching technologies, and upgrades.
Supplies 8 dragoons. Replaces French order dragoons with Portuguese legionnaire dragoons with increased range. \n • Choosing this order blocks access to other orders in this shipment.
Supplies 10 Dutch carabineers. Allows hiring carabineers in the commandery. \n • Choosing this order blocks access to other orders in this shipment.

Supplies 8 Russian horse archers - nobles. Allows hiring nobles in the commandery. \n • Unlocks access to "Bow Raiders" in the arsenal - horse archers learn to attack buildings from afar with fire arrows at increased speed.\n • Choosing this order blocks access to other orders in this shipment.
Supplies 9 French gendarmes. Allows hiring gendarmes in the commandery. \n • Choosing this order blocks access to other orders in this shipment.
Supplies 12 Russian oprichniki. Allows hiring oprichniki in the commandery. \n • Choosing this order blocks access to other orders in this shipment.
Supplies 15 Italian schiavoni. Allows hiring schiavoni in the commandery. \n • Choosing this order blocks access to other orders in this shipment.
Supplies 9 German doppelsoldners. Allows hiring doppelsoldners in the commandery. \n • Choosing this order blocks access to other orders in this shipment.
Supplies 13 Portuguese caçadores. Allows hiring caçadores in the commandery. \n • Choosing this order blocks access to other orders in this shipment.
Supplies 9 Spanish garrochistas. Allows hiring garrochistas in the commandery. \n • Choosing this order blocks access to other orders in this shipment.
Supplies 4 French grenadiers. Allows hiring grenadiers in the commandery. \n • Choosing this order blocks access to other orders in this shipment.
Supplies 11 Spanish rodeleros. Allows hiring rodeleros in the commandery. \n • Choosing this order blocks access to other orders in this shipment.
Supplies 10 English longbowmen. Allows hiring longbowmen in the commandery. \n • Choosing this order blocks access to other orders in this shipment.
Supplies 2 German settler wagons. Allows limited hiring of settler wagons in the commandery. \n • Choosing these immigrants blocks access to other immigrants in this shipment.
Supplies 3 French coureurs des bois. Allows limited hiring of coureurs des bois in the commandery. \n • Choosing these immigrants blocks access to other immigrants in this shipment.
Supplies 3 Sicilian nomads. Allows limited hiring of nomads in the commandery. \n • Choosing these immigrants blocks access to other immigrants in this shipment.
Versatile mercenary cavalry with low health. In melee has full immunity to melee damage, in ranged combat has full immunity to ranged damage. Effective against all units.
Hire a detachment of immortal dragoons
Allows engineers to build stationary guns, powder arsenals, towers, blockhouses, barracks, stables, artillery yards. Engineers now receive bonuses from settler upgrades.
Arrives quickly! Skirmishers gain increased attack strength and health with the start of each age starting from the commerce age.
Skirmisher Trainability: skirmisher attack strength and health increased by 5%
Skirmisher Trainability: skirmisher attack strength and health increased by 10%
Skirmisher Trainability: skirmisher attack strength and health increased by 15%
Skirmisher Trainability: skirmisher attack strength and health increased by 20%
Arrives quickly! Riders gain increased health and melee attack strength with the start of each age starting from the commerce age.
Cavalry Trainability: cavalry attack strength and health increased by 5%
Cavalry Trainability: cavalry attack strength and health increased by 10%
Cavalry Trainability: cavalry attack strength and health increased by 15%
Cavalry Trainability: cavalry attack strength and health increased by 20%
Spear throwers gain increased attack strength and health.
Arrives quickly! Melee infantry gains increased attack strength and health with the start of each age, starting from the commerce age.
Infantry Trainability: melee infantry attack strength and health increased by 5%
Infantry Trainability: melee infantry attack strength and health increased by 10%
Infantry Trainability: melee infantry attack strength and health increased by 15%
Infantry Trainability: melee infantry attack strength and health increased by 20%
Arrives quickly! Pikemen gain increased attack strength and health with the start of each age, starting from the commerce age.
Pikeman Trainability: pikeman attack strength and health increased by 5%
Pikeman Trainability: pikeman attack strength and health increased by 10%
Pikeman Trainability: pikeman attack strength and health increased by 15%
Pikeman Trainability: pikeman attack strength and health increased by 20%
All your spear throwers become soldados.
You and your allies receive a small additional influx of money for the rest of the game. Mines provide resources longer.
Dragoon Trainability: ranged cavalry attack strength and health increased by 5%
Dragoon Trainability: ranged cavalry attack strength and health increased by 10%
Dragoon Trainability: ranged cavalry attack strength and health increased by 15%
Dragoon Trainability: ranged cavalry attack strength and health increased by 20%
Arrives quickly! Mounted shooters gain increased health and ranged attack strength with the start of each age, starting from the commerce age.
Supplies a regiment of counter-dragoons, effective against all cavalry and artillery, and allows creating them in forts.
Supplies a regiment of counter-dragoons, effective against all cavalry and artillery, and allows creating them in stables and fluyts.
Strelets Trainability: firearm infantry attack strength and health increased by 5%
Strelets Trainability: firearm infantry attack strength and health increased by 10%
Strelets Trainability: firearm infantry attack strength and health increased by 15%
Strelets Trainability: firearm infantry attack strength and health increased by 20%

Arrives quickly! Firearm infantry (musketeers and skirmishers) gain increased attack strength and health with the start of each age, starting from the commerce age. Strelets additionally increase damage against heavy infantry.
Restores the original deck and moves you to the imperial age.
Banks increase money production, trader creation limit increases by 10.
Sends a bank wagon that can build a bank. Banks increase money production.
Sends a bank wagon that can build a bank. Bank construction limit increases by 1.
Supplies 3 pawnshop wagons and invests a small amount of various resources in pawnshops, which over time will bring you food, wood, and money.
Supplies a field gun and spear throwers
Supplies 1 spear thrower for each trading post, warrior hut, and noble house.
Supplies 1 royal embassy wagon where you can hire Gustavian guards and upgrade them.
Gustavian halberdiers gain increased attack strength, health, and melee attack range.
Artillery Trainability: artillery attack strength and health increased by 5%
Artillery Trainability: artillery attack strength and health increased by 10%
Artillery Trainability: artillery attack strength and health increased by 15%
Artillery Trainability: artillery attack strength and health increased by 20%
Arrives quickly! Artillery gains increased attack strength and health with the start of each age, starting from the commerce age.
Supplies 10 random Russian riders. Allows training riders in small detachments at a lower cost. Oprichniki learn to attack at a short distance.

Mixed army of Russian riders.
2 Cossacks 
2 Oprichniki 
2 Horse Archers
Supplies 8 landwehrs and 1 siege siphon warrior.
All German landwehrs transform into arquebusiers, and all qiang pikemen transform into changdao swordsmen.
Cho-ko-nu shooters and steppe riders gain increased health.
Landwehrs and steppe riders gain increased health.
Landwehrs and arquebusiers deal additional damage to light cavalry, assault, and heavy infantry.
Landwehrs, qiang pikemen, keshiks, and steppe riders gain significantly increased attack strength and health, but their cost also increases.

Cho-ko-nu shooters are completely replaced with veteran landwehrs in the military academy, home city, and on the map. Landwehrs receive all bonuses that were aimed at improving cho-ko-nu shooters.\n • After ordering, this card will be replaced with an infinite shipment containing 10 landwehrs
Well-balanced army of shooters and pikemen.\n3 German landwehrs: skirmishers effective against infantry.\n3 qiang pikemen: ancient melee infantry effective against riders.
Anti-infantry army of shooters and heavily armed riders.\n3 landwehrs: skirmishers effective against infantry.\n2 steppe riders: ancient melee riders effective against villagers and buildings.
Changdao swordsmen deal area damage.\n • After ordering, this card will be replaced with an infinite shipment containing 14 changdao swordsmen

Bandit units require less population. 5 Penitent Mercenary Iron Warriors are sent to you and your allies, and you can create them in the monastery.\n • After ordering, this card will be replaced with an infinite shipment containing 8 iron warriors

Supplies a detachment of horse archers effective against riders, cavalry, and settlers. All your bow riders increase attack range and gain access to fast long-range siege attacks. Replaces all shipments with keshiks with shipments of Mongol riders. \n • After ordering, this card will be replaced with an infinite shipment containing 7 Mongol riders\n • Mongol riders will also receive bonuses when ordering "Reforms of the Old Dynasty"

All cavalry is trained and moves faster. With each subsequent shipment, an additional French scout arrives.
 • Cavalry: -35% training time
 • Cavalry: +5% speed
 • (Team) Sends 3 royal dragoons.

Supplies a detachment of swordsmen effective against light infantry and artillery. Replaces all shipments with pikemen with shipments of swordsmen. Allows hiring and upgrading detachments of Shaolin monks in the castle. \n • After ordering, this card will be replaced with an infinite shipment containing 10 swordsmen

With each use, supplies fewer siege siphons and resets after three uses.
 • First time: 3 siege siphons
 • Second time: 2 siege siphons
 • Third time: 1 siege siphon
Shaolin monk who shoots quickly and counters riders and mercenaries at a distance. Effective in melee.
(Team) Infantry and settlers move faster, +2 Mongol scouts.
Well-balanced army of heavy and assault infantry.\n3 houyi archers: effective against riders and mercenaries.\n3 warriors with wicker shields: effective against shooters, skirmishers, and artillery.
Supplies heavily armed houyi archers (analogous to musketeers). With each subsequent age, supplies additional archers.
Sends 3 random artillery units. 2 artillery from the list: falconet, culverin, mortar, horse artillery. 1 artillery from the list: heavy cannon, great bombard, rocket.
Supplies 3 royal embassy wagons. Royal embassies start providing passive gold income. Settlers become Sacred Band infantry.\n \nNative settlements can sponsor your revolution!
All native warriors are upgraded to Champion. Sacred Band infantry increases siege attack range and speed.
Supplies a fishing boat for each shipment sent during the game. When researching technologies, you receive a free fishing boat.
Supplies 2 galleys and 1 order galley. Allows training Sacred Band infantry in galleys.

Influential Phanariote dynasties give access to their units. Supplies several Phanariote units for each of your royal embassies, upgrades them to veterans, and allows creating them in the royal embassy. Evzone and Boyar creation limits are increased, but they now occupy population slots. Supplies 1 royal embassy wagon and increases their construction limit by 1.

Influential Hanover dynasty gives access to their units. Supplies several Hanover units for each of your royal embassies, upgrades them to veterans, and allows creating them in the royal embassy. Brunswick and Death Hussar creation limits are increased, but they now occupy population slots. Supplies 1 royal embassy wagon and increases their construction limit by 1.

Influential Wittelsbach dynasty gives access to their units. Supplies several Wittelsbach units for each of your royal embassies, upgrades them to veterans, and allows creating them in the royal embassy. Mountain Soldier and Chevau-léger creation limits are increased, but they now occupy population slots. Supplies 1 royal embassy wagon and increases their construction limit by 1.

Influential Wettin dynasty gives access to their units. Supplies several Wettin units for each of your royal embassies, upgrades them to veterans, and allows creating them in the royal embassy. Drabant and Saxon Cuirassier creation limits are increased, but they now occupy population slots. Supplies 1 royal embassy wagon and increases their construction limit by 1.

Influential Oldenburg dynasty gives access to their units. Supplies several Oldenburg units for each of your royal embassies, upgrades them to veterans, and allows creating them in the royal embassy. Northern Musketeer and Royal Hunter creation limits are increased, but they now occupy population slots. Supplies 1 royal embassy wagon and increases their construction limit by 1.

Influential Habsburg dynasty gives access to their units. Supplies several Habsburg units for each of your royal embassies, upgrades them to veterans, and allows creating them in the royal embassy. Line Infantry and Mounted Infantry creation limits are increased, but they now occupy population slots. Supplies 1 royal embassy wagon and increases their construction limit by 1.
Fast Italian skirmishers effective against heavy infantry / Light artillery effective against artillery

Croatian mounted counter-skirmishers join your revolution and your allies. You and your European allies can hire mounted counter-jägers in stables, forts, commanderies, galleons (galleasses, fluyts, steamships).
Supplies 2 prospector wagons. Prospector wagons can be hired in the town center starting from the industrial age.
Supplies 2 prospector wagons that can open a gold mine.
(Team) Villagers earn money faster at mines, tribal markets, and mountain monasteries.
Supplies 2 factory wagons. Factory wagons become available for creation in the town center. Factories now occupy population slots, have no construction limit, but can no longer produce heavy cannons.
Supplies an army of mercenaries: highlanders, Swiss pikemen (waardgelders), and jägers.

Units from the commandery receive a significant boost to health and attack strength, as if you had advanced to the imperial age.\n • Unit from commandery: +40% health\n • Unit from commandery: +40% unit "All Actions" damage
Dutch supporters supply 16 counter-dragoons from Friesland, already existing reiters are replaced with counter-dragoons. Allows hiring counter-dragoons in the commandery, fort, and steamship.
Converts all your town centers into fort wagons. Increases fort construction limit by +3. Town centers are cheaper but take longer to build.
Stables become commanderies, and each shipment brings an additional commandery wagon. Settlers become bogenschützen with reduced damage multipliers but increased base damage.
Automatically produces resources of the player's choice. Can train factory wagons in the town center.

Supplies 3 bogenschützen for every 2 shipments sent during the game. For each shipment sent as part of Belgium, an additional 1 bogenschützen is supplied, but the cost of "professional training" will be increased. \nFirearm infantry (musketeers) increases health and attack strength, hiring cost of bogenschützen increases.

Spanish supporters supply 6 Caballero Pesada (gendarmes) and 6 Conquistadors, already existing cuirassier-gendarmes become caballeros, already existing dragoons and war wagons are replaced with conquistadors. Allows hiring caballeros and conquistadors in the commandery, fort, and steamship. Conquistador starts counting as a unit from the commandery, not a native warrior.
Supplies 4 settler wagons. Allows creating settler wagons in the town center for food and gold. Allows creating boats in the seaport.

French supporters supply 15 "Old Guard" grenadiers, already existing grenadiers become old guard. Allows hiring old guard in the commandery, fort, and steamship, replaces grenadiers in the artillery foundry with old guard.

British supporters supply 12 independence rangers, already existing spies are replaced with independence rangers. Allows hiring rangers in the commandery, fort, and steamship, replaces spies in the church with rangers.
German supporters supply 12 Prussian uhlans, already existing uhlans and hussars are replaced with Prussian uhlans. Allows hiring uhlans in the commandery, fort, and steamship.
Assault infantry capable of hiding. Effective against mercenaries, heroes, artillery, heavy cavalry, and heavy infantry. Vulnerable to light infantry and light cavalry.
Villagers gather food faster, and you can now create settler wagons at mills, +3 uhlans.

Supplies 3 bogenschützen for every 2 military production buildings you own (up to 21). Allows bogenschützen to build commanderies and forts. Soldiers fighting near your forts now gain twice as much experience.
Supplies a powerful battleship! Greek battleships can now rain fire on enemy ships and buildings at close range.
Passive ability: deals fire damage at close range. Burning damage after defeating a target deals additional periodic damage.

Heavy shooter armed with a tüfek spear and throwing knives, effective against heavy infantry and cavalry. Spear attacks in melee deal area damage and speed up when health is low. Knife throwing speeds up depending on distance to the target. \nReflects part of the damage dealt to nearby allies, and restores health when idle.
Temporarily increases the speed of all houyi archers, switches them to melee mode, and prevents enemies from slowing them.

Revolutionary. Heavy ranged infantry, deals higher base damage at the cost of lower additional damage, effective against riders. Gains one and a half times more experience for killing enemy units and destroying buildings.

Homeland sends 23 counter-jägers (light infantry effective against other light infantry). Allows your counter-jägers to gain combat promotions, additionally increasing attack range and damage for each enemy military unit destroyed. In all shipments, oprichniki are replaced with counter-jägers.
This unit gains a promotion for each enemy military unit destroyed. Its damage and attack radius increase.
Supplies an additional carabineer each time you send this shipment (starting from 9).
Supplies an additional musketeer each time you send this shipment (starting from 14).
Supplies additional Canadian lumberjacks each time you send this shipment (starting from 19).

Yermak Timofeyevich's expedition made an unpopular decision!\nEach shipment additionally supplies a Manchu. Villagers become keshiks, and houses become Mongol archers. Nobles in stables are replaced with Mongol archers
Supplies 2 village rickshaws, 5 settlers, and 5 buffalo. Allows creating settlers, keshiks, and buffalo in villages. Villages can now exchange livestock for wood or gold.
Increases village health and raises the maximum population limit to 250.
All your recruits become houyi archers. Each shipment starts supplying an additional houyi, the effect can stack.
All your lieutenants become changdao swordsmen. Each shipment starts supplying an additional changdao, the effect can stack.
All your streltsy become cho-ko-nu. Each shipment starts supplying an additional cho-ko-nu, the effect can stack.
All your oprichniki become meteor hammer riders. Each shipment starts supplying an additional meteor hammer, the effect can stack.
All your Cossacks become iron flail riders. Each shipment starts supplying an additional iron flail, the effect can stack.
All your horse archers become keshiks. Supplies 1 keshik for each transformed horse archer. Each shipment starts supplying an additional keshik, the effect can stack.
Allows researching imperial upgrades for Asian units in the village at a reduced cost. In addition to the main stat improvements, units also receive unique bonuses.
Supplies 1 monastery rickshaw. Monastery and church increase the influx of received experience.
All your keshiks become Tatar archers! Allows limited training of Tatar archers and other Asian units in the castle.
All your horse archers gain increased attack strength and health. Keshiks increase damage dealt to heavy cavalry. Tatar archers increase shooting range.
Cho-ko-nu shooters gain increased attack strength and health. Shooters increase damage dealt to light cavalry, heavy and assault infantry.
Changdao swordsmen gain increased attack strength and health. Swordsmen deal area damage in melee.
Houyi archers gain increased attack strength and health. Melee attack strength increases to a greater extent.
Iron flail riders gain increased attack strength and health. Iron flail riders deal more damage to shooters and skirmishers.
Meteor hammer riders gain increased attack strength and health. Meteor hammer riders increase damage dealt to artillery and heavy cavalry.
Supplies several waffenmänner and assault grenadiers.
Irregular Manchu rider, effective against riders. Has increased damage against settlers and increased shooting range compared to a regular horse archer.

Settlers become fianna - settlers who gather gold more efficiently than other resources. 
Barracks become mercenary camps. 
 
Health and attack strength of mercenaries are significantly increased.

Converts manors into thorps and allows their construction. Thorps become stronger, can accept economic shipments from the home city, and gather resources from crates. Allows selling thorps to partially recover the resources spent on them.

Replaces the hiring cost of infantry with an equivalent in gold and allows creating them in mercenary camps. Allows researching imperial upgrades for units in barracks, but these upgrades will be less effective. Infantry upgrades no longer require wood.

Irish revolutionary-settler, capable of fending off heroes and efficiently gathering treasures. Produces a small amount of experience while gathering resources, gathers gold significantly faster, other resources are gathered slower.
Supplies 2 highlanders, 2 jägers, and 3 mounted arquebusiers. Allows hiring highlanders, jägers, and mounted arquebusiers in the mercenary camp and pub.
Supplies 4 waffenmänner, 7 assault grenadiers, and 7 Vyatkin brigadiers. Allows hiring waffenmänner, Hamelin grenadiers, and Vyatkin brigadiers in the mercenary camp and pub.
Mercenaries from different ages gain increased attack strength and health. Allows creating mercenaries in groups of 10, but all military units train slower.
Supplies 1 highlander for every 2 military production buildings you own (up to 17).
All your fianna become brigadiers to fight for Ireland! Upgrades Irish brigadiers to "wild geese," increasing damage dealt to cavalry at a distance.
Mercenaries are trained and move faster. Mercenary health slowly restores—even in battle.
Supplies 1 royal embassy wagon where you can hire Gustavian guards (halberdiers, native warriors). Allows creating fianna in the royal embassy.
Upgrades saloons to pubs with adventurers. Supplies 2 pub builder wagons and increases pub construction limit. Additionally, bandits occupy fewer population slots, increase damage dealt, and health.
Thorps become stronger, can accept economic shipments from the home city, and gather resources from crates. Allows selling thorps to partially recover the resources spent on them. + 4 thorp wagons
Supplies 1 royal halberdier for every military production building you own (up to 17). Upgrades halberdiers to guards.
Supplies 1 halberdier for every military production building you own (up to 17). Upgrades halberdiers to guards.
Arrives quickly! All your villagers, halberdiers, pikemen, and reiters join the rebellious Flemish militia and become counter-dragoons!
Commerce age infantry gains a bonus to attack radius and becomes available in town centers
Gives explorers access to the "Hot Air Balloon" ability
4 veteran grenadiers \n \nAllows training grenadiers and supplies them with hand mortars
Spies, rangers, and scouts gain increased attack strength and health, as well as damage dealt to mercenaries, bandits, and heroes.
(Team) Sends a detachment of Hamelin waffenmänner for the entire team.
Hospitals restore more health. Maltese units are trained faster.
Supplies 1 church wagon. Increases church work speed, experience production, and construction limit.
Oprichniki gain increased health, and they can be hired in the commerce age.
Increases town center construction limit by 2. Town centers cost less wood.
Each town center or settlement can summon 2 Asian buffalo at a time. Villages can now exchange livestock for wood or gold.
Supplies 2 privateer ships to your city, allows creating them at the dock.

Maygadi are trained faster, their attack strength increases. From now on, they must be paid partially in money and influence. To recover the cost, the shipment can be sent again.
 • Maygadi: +20% unit "All Actions" damage
 • Maygadi: -25% training time
 • Maygadi: adds 100.00 to "Money" resource cost
 • Maygadi: brings "Influence" resource cost to 90.00
Replaces the cost of Maghadi in money with the equivalent in influence.\n • Maghadi: adds 100.00 to the cost of the "Influence" resource\n • Maghadi: reduces the cost of the "Money" resource to 0.00
Allows all mounted archers to attack buildings from a distance with fire arrows, which gradually inflict burning damage.
Light infantry deals increased damage to heavy infantry. Anti-skirmishers increase damage dealt to light infantry. Abus guns increase damage against all infantry.
Azaps gain increased attack strength and range, as well as increased health. Grants access to a rechargeable spear attack effective against all cavalry.
Provides 1 organ for each of your town centers.
Allows hiring cannoneers and small bombards in the tavern
7 trained bears\n \nUpgrades bears to shock infantry effective against artillery

Bonus: −10% to gold costs for hiring mercenaries

Creates: incendiaries, Jat lancers, haramii, camel gatlings

Provides: mercenary buildings

Bonus: −10% to gold costs for hiring mercenaries

Creates: Hameln grenadiers, iron troops, Manchus, yojimbo avengers

Provides: mercenary buildings

Provides 4 wagons for the tavern, which can build mercenary camps. Mercenary camps have less health and export production compared to saloons but train mercenaries much faster (allows creating mercenary camps via military wagons ordered in castles).

Provides 1 wagon for the saloon. Saloons support 20 population units, instead of gold, saloons produce export. Increases the saloon build limit (allows creating saloons via military wagons ordered in castles).

Provides 4 wagons for the tavern, which can build mercenary camps. Mercenary camps have less health and export production compared to saloons but train mercenaries much faster (allows settlers to build mercenary camps).
Provides 1 wagon for the saloon. Saloons support 20 population units, instead of gold, saloons produce export. Increases the saloon build limit (allows settlers to build saloons).
Lightly armed Asian cavalry, effective against cavalry and artillery. Vulnerable to light infantry.\n • After death, becomes a vengeful spirit.
Yojimbo without a horse. Shock light melee infantry, effective against cavalry and artillery, vulnerable to light infantry. Gradually loses health.
Peasants and bandits gain increased health. Bandit units require fewer population slots, and their upgrades become cheaper.
Bandits are created faster. Allows creating strong colonial bandit settlers for influence. Replaces the cost of hiring desert bandits with the equivalent in influence.
Colonial bandit with a revolver, effective against cavalry. Can perform a carbine shot similar to dragoon fire. When mounted, transforms into a mounted shooter.
Colonial bandit with a revolver, effective against cavalry. His horse was killed, so he can no longer become a dragoon or fire a carbine.

Colonial bandit armed with a carbine, effective against cavalry, artillery, and animals. Can use a rechargeable revolver shot and dismount, becoming a heavy foot shooter. If killed while mounted, continues fighting as a revolver bandit with reduced health but loses the ability to mount or fire a carbine.
Colonial bandit with a sword, effective against cavalry. Periodically throws dynamite at enemies and buildings.
Colonial bandit with a rifle, anti-skirmisher with high base damage. Effective against light cavalry and infantry, especially skirmishers.
All infantry trains faster.\n • (Shipment) Sends 3 royal musketeers.
Sends 3 colonial oppressors + 1 mamluk. \nUpgrades taverns to theaters, which feature African colonial bandits. Also reduces the population cost for bandits.
All your peasants become irregular mounted archers!
Provides 3 wagons for the saloon. Saloons support 20 population units, instead of gold, saloons produce export. Increases the saloon build limit (allows settlers to build saloons).

Bonus: -25% to shipment arrival speed

Creates: carabineers, snipers, regular soldiers, gatlings

Provides: American buildings
Provides a large number of state militia. All light infantry increases damage dealt to heavy infantry and light cavalry.
Summons a minutemen unit. Replaces irregular soldiers and sentries in the town center and castle with "Summon Minutemen"
Provides 6 winged hussars. Allows hiring winged hussars in military buildings. Winged hussars now occupy population slots but have no creation limit.
Provides 9 northern musketeers. Allows hiring northern musketeers in military buildings at the cost of regular musketeers. Northern musketeers now occupy population slots but have no creation limit.
Crossbowmen gain increased attack strength but now have reduced health.
Provides 11 royal arquebusiers. Allows hiring royal arquebusiers in military buildings. Royal arquebusiers now occupy population slots but have no creation limit.
All local warriors are upgraded to guards.
Peasants gather wood 20% faster. Refunds wood for each building constructed (up to 1500 resources).
Order summon: call 10 crossbowmen for defense.
Order summon: call 10 pikemen for defense.
Order summon: call 8 skirmishers for defense.
Order summon: call 8 doppelsoldners for defense.
Provides 17 line infantry. Allows hiring line infantry in military buildings. Line infantry now occupies population slots but has no creation limit.
Sends 2 knight scouts for each military camp (up to 9), +1 knight scout.

Mountain troops, death hussars, and chevaulegers can be hired in military buildings. Allows building a capitol and researching most technologies without advancing to the imperial age. Also, "Legendary Native Warriors" become cheaper.
Sends 3 knight scouts for each town center and trading post (up to 18).
Sends 1 knight scout for every 2 buildings (up to 20).
Builds quickly. Supports 10 population units. Allows researching unit upgrades.
Provides 5 order skirmishers and 5 order doppelsoldners. Allows periodically summoning pikemen and crossbowmen units from combat wagons.

Provides 5 order skirmishers and 5 order doppelsoldners. Allows periodically summoning skirmishers and doppelsoldners from military camps, tents, and combat wagons. Pikemen and doppelsoldner summons share a cooldown, similar to crossbowmen and skirmisher summons.
Native warriors, embassies, and factory technologies become cheaper. Allows building native embassies.
Teutonic heavy infantry with a large shield, armed with a crossbow and spear. Depending on the shield's position, it provides different types of protection. Effective against cavalry and buildings.

Knight scouts deal more damage to light cavalry at range. Teutonic crossbowmen shoot farther and deal more damage to heavy infantry. All crossbow infantry can attack buildings from a distance with fire bolts that gradually inflict burning damage.
Knight scouts gain increased attack strength and health.
Knight peacekeepers gain increased attack strength and health.
All your pikemen are converted to doppelsoldners.

An explorer who creates commanders, researches, fights, builds camps, and factories. Cannot die. If he falls in battle, he can be rescued.\n • Starting from the colonial age, he has a chance to deal critical damage in battle.
All military units are created faster, "order summon" can be used more frequently.\n • Order summon: cooldown -33%
Provides 2 additional skirmishers each time you send this shipment (starting from 10).
Provides 1 additional doppelsoldner each time you send this shipment (starting from 6).

Teutonic order commander, uses various passive auras—can increase movement speed, damage, health, experience gained, siege damage, as well as heal or absorb damage. Initially a heavy melee infantry, effective against cavalry.\n • Upon advancing to the industrial age, commanders choose a specialization that can completely change the unit type.
Increases attack strength and health for commanders and order units.
TOC SPD 1% per commander, effects stack
2 settlers, cargo with 100 food\n\nProvides a passive food trickle (+1.5) for the rest of the game.
TOC HP 1% per commander, effects stack
2 settlers, cargo with 100 wood\n\nProvides a passive wood trickle (+1.25) for the rest of the game.
TOC EXP 5% per commander, effects stack
2 settlers, cargo with 100 gold\n\nProvides a passive gold trickle (+1.65) for the rest of the game.
2 settlers, cargo with 100 XP\n\nProvides a passive XP trickle (+1.5) for the rest of the game.
1 covered wagon and books with 300 XP\n\n+5% to land unit health
1 covered wagon and books with 300 XP\n\n+5% to ranged damage for land units
1 covered wagon and books with 300 XP\n\n+5% to melee damage for land units
1 covered wagon and books with 300 XP\n\n+5% to land unit movement speed
1 covered wagon and books with 300 XP\n\n-10% to land unit creation speed

6 order commanders

Order commanders are armed with muskets and become musketeers

+10% to land unit health

6 order commanders

Order commanders are armed with rifled barrels and become skirmishers

+10% to ranged damage for land units

6 order commanders

Order commanders are armed with two-handed swords and deal area damage

+10% to melee damage for land units

6 order commanders

Order commanders are armed with pistols and become shock infantry

+10% to land unit movement speed

6 order commanders

Order commanders are armed with hand mortars and become grenadiers

-15% to land unit creation speed
600 food, 600 wood, 600 gold, 600 XP\n\n+15% to land unit health
2 fort wagons\n\n+15% to ranged damage for land units
17 death hussar guards, allows creating death hussars\n\n+15% to melee damage for land units
+50% to mercenary attack strength and health\n\n+15% to land unit movement speed
1 factory wagon\n\n-20% to land unit creation speed
+50% to mercenary attack strength and health
TOC DMG 1% per commander, effects stack
TOC DEF 25% damage, effects do not stack
TOC SIEGE 1.5% per commander, effects stack
TOC HEAL 5/s per commander, effects stack
Healers and commanders are created and heal faster. Tents now repair nearby buildings.
Villagers become cheaper and now earn additional gold when gathering natural resources, particularly resources from berry bushes are fully replaced with gold, and trees provide resources much longer.

Provides 5 leather cannons and upgrades them to infantry guns. Leather cannons and their imperial upgrade are available in the artillery foundry. Leather cannons are created faster and can deal devastating damage to units in melee combat.
Enhances the combat capabilities of the landmeister, increases damage against artillery, and gives him a powerful pet. When stunned, the landmeister creates a boar.
Sends 1 boar for every 2 commanders fallen in battle.
Affectionate but rough boar, loves back scratches. Effective against treasure guardians, mercenaries, bandits, native warriors, and heroes.
Prussian shooters gain increased attack strength and health. Hiring cost is split between food and gold.
Order summon: call 10 landwehr for defense.

Provides 10 landwehr and 1 outpost wagon, each shipment brings an additional outpost wagon. Ancient order infantry becomes landwehr, and settlers become Prussian shooters—revolutionaries who are both skirmishers and musketeers. Landwehr and Prussian shooters can be upgraded to imperial in tents.
Prussian universal infantry, effective against heavy infantry and cavalry, especially light.
Outposts can summon order units, "order summon" can be used more frequently.\n • Order summon: cooldown -25%
Order summon: call 10 musketeers for defense.
Provides 15 peacekeepers and uhlans in random proportions, allows training uhlans in outposts, military camps, galleons, and forts. Allows training peacekeepers in outposts.
Provides 20 scouts and musketeers in random proportions, allows training scouts in outposts. Order summon—pikemen, is replaced with musketeer summon.
Order summon: call 10 musketeers for defense.
Summon: call Prussian shooters to defend this town!
Revolutionaries gain increased attack strength and health.
Native warrior trainability: native warrior attack strength and health increased by 5%
Native warrior trainability: native warrior attack strength and health increased by 10%
Native warrior trainability: native warrior attack strength and health increased by 15%
Native warrior trainability: native warrior attack strength and health increased by 20%
Arrives quickly! Native warriors gain increased attack strength and health at the start of each age, beginning with the commerce age.
Provides 1 manor wagon. New and existing mills and manors create 2 settlers and start producing a small trickle of resources.
Reduces the population cost for bandits, also allows hiring powerful colonial bandits in taverns.
Mercenaries gain increased attack strength and health. Stradiots and Bosniaks are available for hire in taverns and barracks.
Early falconet. In the fortress age, sending this shipment becomes free.
Provides 1 falconet and 1 mortar. Falconets and mortars can be created in blockhauses. Moves mortars and horse artillery to the fortress age.
Provides 1 military wagon. Settlers gather food from hunting faster near your blockhauses or other outposts.\n • Settler: +20% to food gathering efficiency
Provides 1 military wagon. Settlers gather wood faster near your blockhauses or other outposts.\n • Settler: +20% to wood gathering efficiency
Provides 1 military wagon. Settlers gather gold from mines faster near your blockhauses or other outposts.\n • Settler: +20% to gold gathering efficiency
Reduces the cost of all cavalry, especially lisowczyks. Cavalry upgrades are researched significantly faster.
Blockhauses increase line of sight and attack range, and support an additional 5 population.
Balkan lightly armed shooter, effective against heavy infantry. Deals higher base damage due to lower additional damage.
Macedonian swordsman armed with a sword, spear, and grenades. Effective against buildings, native warriors, and melee units, including infantry and cavalry.
Cheap Montenegrin musketeer with a small attack radius but increased health and movement speed. Effective against cavalry at range and in melee.
Heavily armed cavalry with a large line of sight, effective against infantry and artillery. Occupies fewer population slots due to reduced health and base damage.

Provides several dobroant musketeers. At the start of a new age, this card is restored, and the number of dobroants provided increases. In the imperial age, this card becomes completely infinite but sends fewer units than in the industrial age.

Provides several mounted archers. At the start of a new age, this card is restored, and the number of mounted archers provided increases. In the imperial age, this card becomes completely infinite but sends fewer units than in the industrial age.

Provides several Balkan skirmishers. At the start of a new age, this card is restored, and the number of skirmishers provided increases. In the imperial age, this card becomes completely infinite but sends fewer units than in the industrial age.

Provides several Macedonian swordsmen (swordsmen). At the start of a new age, this card is restored, and the number of swordsmen provided increases. In the imperial age, this card becomes completely infinite but sends fewer units than in the industrial age.

Supplies several Roshior Dragoons. At the start of a new age, this card is restored, and the number of supplied Dragoons increases. In the Imperial Age, this card becomes completely infinite but sends fewer units than in the Industrial Age.
Increases the attack strength and hit points of melee infantry.
Increases the attack strength and hit points of melee cavalry.
Increases the attack strength and hit points of ranged infantry.
Increases the attack strength and hit points of ranged cavalry.
Resource crates from shipments are sent directly to your treasury and provide more resources.
(Team) Natural resources (wild animals, berry bushes, trees, mines, as well as cherry orchards, mangrove groves, and mountain monasteries) provide resources for longer.
Supplies 1 revolutionary for each shipment sent. Federal unit shipments send additional units.
2 settlers, 1 estate wagon\n\nBalkan Sharpshooter: status set to "Available"
1 covered wagon, 1 estate wagon\n\nBalkan Sharpshooter: status set to "Available"

Increases the attack strength and hit points of national melee units.
Increases the attack strength of warships but decreases their hit points.
Delivery of the Assembly Outpost. Also increases the line of sight of outposts.
Reduces the cost of all pikemen and large buttons with Swiss Pikemen.
Slowly arriving shipment of 400 food and coin.
Increases the attack strength and hit points of Alpini.
Swiss heavy infantry deals more damage to cavalry and shock infantry.
Alpini draw pistols to fend off heavy infantry and fire rifles to counter artillery.
Arrives slowly! Provides a small trickle of experience for the rest of the game. Temporarily stops all battles.
1 military wagon and 1 mounted crossbowman\n\nAllows training mounted crossbowmen
2 coureurs des bois and 1 settler wagon
Mints coins directly into your treasury.
Supplies 1 prospector wagon, which opens a random mine.
Alpini now gain benefits from natural resources and slowly regenerate hit points.
Allows building more outposts, but they become less durable.
Your crossbowmen become mountain soldiers. Mountain soldiers now occupy population slots. They can be upgraded in the royal embassy and trained in the royal embassy, outpost, castle, and galleass.
Mobilization! All workers arm themselves with muskets to defend settlements. New workers stop appearing.

Martial law is over. All irregular soldiers become workers. "Mobilization" becomes available again.\nIf mobilization is not ended manually, it becomes automatically available at the start of a new age.
Veteran and guard upgrades for infantry become free but take significantly longer to research.
Adds a free Swiss pikeman to all your past and future shipments. All shipments with regular pikemen are restored and now send Swiss pikemen, but they arrive significantly slower.
Supplies 1 market wagon, increases the building limit for markets. Allows creating 10 crates at a time.
Supplies a castle wagon, allowing the construction of a powerful mountain castle.
Supplies a castle wagon, allowing the construction of a powerful mountain castle. Explorer and Alpini gain access to building castles and outposts.
Alpini move faster and gain increased hit points.
Increases the attack strength and hit points of pikemen but significantly reduces their speed.
Supplies 4 sappers, sappers are available one age earlier.
Supplies 8 sappers, sappers improve grenade attacks.
Supplies 15 sappers, sappers increase melee splash radius.
Foot crossbowmen fire farther and deal more damage to heavy infantry. Mounted crossbowmen deal full damage to heavy cavalry and gain a rechargeable spear area attack.
Arrives quickly! Supplies an additional dragoon each time you send this card (starting from 10).
Supplies 1 maritime trading company wagon, increases their building limit. Warship alliances arrive faster.
All fishing boats slowly heal nearby ships.
Supplies 1 military trading company wagon, increases their building limit. Artillery shipments arrive faster, factories produce heavy cannons faster.
12 besteiro\n\nCrossbowmen upgrade to guards, allows researching imperial crossbowman upgrade
+25% to attack strength and hit points of native warriors\n\nLegendary Native Warriors: researched
Increases the maximum population limit by 20
Allows hiring jaegers and royal cavalry in taverns, barracks, and stables
1 hot air balloon, +4 to balloon line of sight
Supplies 1 livestock pen wagon, increases their building limit. All livestock gains weight faster, cows are available in livestock pens.
Supplies 2 infrastructure wagons. Mills, livestock pens, and trading companies increase the building limit, as well as cost less and build faster.
Supplies 1 mill wagon, increases their building limit. Mills provide a small trickle of food.
Supplies 1000 food. All food crates now contain 25% more resources.
Supplies 1000 wood. All wood crates now contain 25% more resources.
Irregular Swiss soldier serving as a musketeer, effective against cavalry. Can construct some buildings, gather treasures, and collect resources from crates. Cannot be healed.
Temporarily increases food gathering speed by 35% and speeds up worker movement by 25%.
Temporarily increases wood gathering speed by 35% and speeds up building construction by 25%.
Upgrades all Russian military units to imperial status.
Upgrades all Russian military units to guard status.
Upgrades all Russian military units to veteran status.

Supplies influence. The amount increases with each age:
 • Discovery Age: 300 influence
 • Commerce Age: 600 influence
 • Fortress Age: 900 influence
 • Industrial Age: 1300 influence
 • Imperial Age: 1600 influence

Supplies coin crates. The amount increases with each age:
 • Commerce Age: 600 coin
 • Fortress Age: 900 coin
 • Industrial Age: 1300 coin
 • Imperial Age: 1600 coin

Supplies wood crates. The amount increases with each age:
 • Commerce Age: 500 wood
 • Fortress Age: 900 wood
 • Industrial Age: 1200 wood
 • Imperial Age: 1600 wood

Supplies food crates. The amount increases with each age:
 • Commerce Age: 600 food
 • Fortress Age: 900 food
 • Industrial Age: 1300 food
 • Imperial Age: 1600 food
Unique Swiss bonus of +10% to infantry siege damage
Peasants produce food much faster at mills. The building limit for mills increases by 2.
Special forces shock infantry, effective against heavy cavalry. Can temporarily switch to pistol mode, becoming effective against heavy infantry and artillery. Gains a damage bonus in groups.
Mounted ranged unit, effective against heavy infantry, light cavalry, and artillery. Vulnerable to light infantry and light cavalry.
Supplies 1 outpost wagon, increases their building limit. Supplies 5 random Swiss military units.
Swiss light infantryman using a rifle and grenades at close range. Effective against heavy infantry, light cavalry, and buildings, and in melee against artillery. Vulnerable to heavy cavalry.

Hero of your civilization. Explores, fights, builds town centers and factories. Can die. Gathers natural resources and generates influence.
Upgrades all German military units to imperial status.
Upgrades all German military units to guard status.
Upgrades all German military units to veteran status.
Houses gather less food, coin, and wood but more influence
Allows further strengthening of consulate units from one country.
All Russian units receive an additional upgrade to attack strength and hit points. Post-imperial upgrades for Germans and Swedes become unavailable.
All German units receive an additional upgrade to attack strength and hit points. Post-imperial upgrades for Russians and Swedes become unavailable.
All Swedish units receive an additional upgrade to attack strength and hit points. Post-imperial upgrades for Germans and Russians become unavailable.
All houses turn into torp wagons.
Supplies 5 counterdragoons, allows training counterdragoons, hakkapeliittas, military wagons, and horse archers in the Commerce Age.
Supplies 1 death hussar for every 3 lost melee cavalry, and replaces all existing uhlans, hussars, and cossacks with guard death hussars, using only 1 population slot.
Supplies 10 scouts. Spies are completely replaced by scouts in churches, the home city, and on the map. Spy shipments are restored, scouts can be trained in wagons, galleons, and forts.

Resends half the units from expeditionary shipments (Blue Coats, Fencing Masters, Garrochistas, Legionary Dragoons, Gendarmes, Old Guard). Units from expeditionary shipments can be trained in supply wagons, forts, and galleons.
Allows re-researching unit upgrades from expeditionary shipments.
This unit gains a boost for each enemy military unit destroyed. Its health and movement speed increase.
Supplies 1 monitor. Moves monitors to the Fortress Age.
Sturdy Finnish horses increase the hit points of all consulate cavalry and blockhouse units.
Increases the attack speed and movement speed of heavy infantry.
Arrives quickly! Supplies 6 militia, who can now be trained in houses for influence. Militia no longer lose health.
Military wagon-barracks, trains consulate units. Can garrison infantry for transport and protection. Becomes faster and stronger the more units are garrisoned inside.
Gain 500 influence. Also, relations are very cheap and research faster.
Destroyed enemy infantry and cavalry provide a small amount of influence in addition to experience.
(Team) Buildings increase damage dealt and hit points.
Supplies units and reduces the cost of Swedish veteran and guard upgrades.
Supplies units and reduces the cost of German veteran and guard upgrades.
Supplies units and reduces the cost of Russian veteran and guard upgrades.
Supplies units and reduces the cost of imperial Swedish upgrades.
Supplies units and reduces the cost of imperial German upgrades.
Supplies units and reduces the cost of imperial Russian upgrades.

Supplies units depending on the chosen consulate alliance:
 • from Russians: 13 strelets
 • from Swedes: 8 crossbowmen 
 • from Germans: 8 landwehrs

Supplies units depending on the chosen consulate alliance:
 • from Russians: 5 cossacks
 • from Swedes: 3 hussars 
 • from Germans: 3 uhlans

Supplies units depending on the chosen alliance in the consulate:
 • Russians: 5 lieutenants
 • Swedes: 8 pikemen 
 • Germans: 3 doppelsoldners

Supplies units depending on the chosen alliance in the consulate:
 • Russians: 6 recruits
 • Swedes: 6 caroleans 
 • Germans: 4 Vyatka brigadiers
Supplies 1 military wagon and 300 influence. Increases the blockhouse's hit points and attack damage.
Units train faster, and supply wagons train them even faster. All wagons move faster, build faster, and increase line of sight.

Supplies 2 artillery tower wagons and consulate units depending on the chosen alliance in the consulate:
 • Russians: 2 mounted archers
 • Swedes: 2 hakkapeliitas 
 • Germans: 1 war wagon
Adds infinite shipments containing resource crates to the deck. The amount of resources sent increases with each age.
Adds infinite shipments with natural resources to the deck.

Settlers become available in houses. All market upgrades are researched, and already researched technologies refund a portion of resources in influence equivalent. Karelian jaegers no longer count as settlers; they can still gather resources but no longer receive settler upgrade bonuses.
Your caravels gain increased hit points and gain the ability for long-range bombardment.
Increases the pioneer build limit, but pioneers can no longer gather resources. Houses' passive influence generation increases.
Replaces artillery cost with an equivalent in influence and more wood.
1500 XP, each subsequent research costs 100 more resources.
Increases the number of free-spawning units and their spawn rate

Militia receive equipment effective against <color=0.72, 0.784, 0.784>heavy cavalry</color>. Does not change the militia's own tags. At the start of the next age, militia equipment can be changed (changing equipment in the imperial age takes VERY long).
Every seventh attack is guaranteed to deal critical damage.

Barracks that automatically produce combined units. When each of the seven possible academies is built for the first time, you receive several random units from the town center.\n • After completing all possible hit point or attack damage upgrades, an additional upgrade becomes available, increasing the attack damage or hit points of all military units at the cost of a small penalty.
This academy trains 1 paladin and 2 priests.
This academy trains 2 warriors and 2 scouts.
This academy trains 2 scouts and 2 rogues.
This academy trains 2 barbarians and 2 warriors.
This academy trains 2 warriors and 2 rogues.
This academy trains 2 warriors and 2 priests.
All warriors equip axes, increasing attack speed and enabling critical damage.
All warriors equip swords, increasing movement speed and enabling critical damage. Ranged attacks are temporarily unavailable.
Ships a crate of 200 XP. All your future shipments include a crate of 200 XP.
Ships a crate of 200 XP. For each subsequent technology researched, a crate of 200 XP is shipped.
Melee assault infantry. Effective against light infantry and artillery. Can deal critical damage. Equipping a bow turns them into ranged assault infantry.
Ranged assault infantry. Effective against cavalry and artillery. Can deal critical damage. Equipping a sword and dagger turns them into melee assault infantry.
Ranged light infantry. Effective against heavy infantry and light cavalry. Periodically throws a knife that can deal critical damage. Can tame treasure guardians.
Ranged heavy infantry. Effective against cavalry in melee and only against heavy cavalry at range. Can temporarily equip other weapons, enabling critical damage.
Melee infantry. Effective against other infantry and buildings. Can heal allies.
Melee heavy infantry. Very slow, effective against cavalry and buildings. Periodically deals siege damage that can be critical.
Melee heavy infantry. Deals area damage, effective against artillery. Using rechargeable abilities can deal critical damage.
Each shipment includes a random crate with 100 resources; settlers gather resources from crates three times faster.
Light infantry (Scouts) deal increased damage to heavy infantry. Ranged cavalry and assault infantry (Rogues with bows) increase attack range.
All military units gain hit points, slightly reducing attack damage.
All military units gain attack damage, slightly reducing hit points.
Ships 2 caballero pesado (heavy cavalry, similar to cuirassiers). Ships an additional caballero each time you send this card. The shipment cost increases each time.

Each time ships different Spanish units in the following order:
 • 16 tercio pikemen
 • 8 garrochistas
 • 14 rodeleros
Mercenaries' hit points and attack damage are significantly increased
Consulate units from shipments gain increased hit points and attack damage. Consulate unit shipments are restored.
(Team) Ships additional kunoichi each time you send this card (starting with 5).
(Team) Ancient infantry's hit points and attack damage are increased.\n • All Outremer infantry, except paladins, are considered ancient.
Unlocks access to 3 randomly selected mercenaries.
Allows training surgeons. Missionaries, surgeons, and battle priests improve healing skills. Paladins improve self-healing.
Academies spawn free squads faster and increase the build limit.
Allows mounted archers and scouts to attack buildings from a distance with fire arrows that deal burning damage over time.

Advancing to the next age is done as a shipment from the home city. Upon completing the age-up, a military wagon arrives from the town center, and the age-up provides one shipment.\n • The town center can continue training settlers.\n • New shipments from the home city are queued after the age-up.
Mercenaries cost less and train faster.
Produces gold. Produces additional gold for each nearby settler.
Villagers gather resources from mines and trees 30% faster near churches and the cathedral.
Ships 2 church wagons and increases their build limit.
And behold, a pale horse, and he who sat on it, his name was Death\n • Your death from a hole in the head.
Produces XP. Contains military upgrades. Heals consulate units nearby.
Settlers train faster and gather food and coin much faster but gather wood slower.
Ships 2 factories. Factories occupy less population. Factories become more durable, and factory wagons move faster.
Allows musketeers to build commanderies and forts. Soldiers fighting near your forts now gain twice as much XP.
Assault heavy infantry with rifles. Effective against infantry. Vulnerable to heavy infantry and light cavalry.
Each house slowly produces 1 settler. Roger's rangers are available in commanderies, steamboats, and forts.
Ships a bank wagon. Stadswachts are available in commanderies, steamboats, and forts.\n • If the bank is lost, it can be rebuilt via a factory wagon.
Each mill slowly produces 1 settler wagon. Settler wagons can be trained at mills. Uhlans are available in commanderies, steamboats, and forts.
Ships a hacienda wagon and 3 galleons. Galleons are available at docks and can train units similarly to steamboats.\n • If the hacienda is lost, it can be rebuilt via a factory wagon.
Ships 2 outpost wagons. Outposts can be built; each outpost trains 1 Hungarian grenadier. Hungarian grenadiers are available in commanderies, steamboats, and forts.
Ships 2 town center wagons and increases their build limit. Town centers slowly produce all resources. Açores are available in commanderies, steamboats, and forts.
(Team) Economic building upgrade technologies (mill, estate, farm, field, rice paddy, pen, market, factory, and fishing boat upgrades) cost 30% less.
Commandery build limit increased. Commanderies build faster and repair for free.
Ships 1 commandery wagon. Commanderies train units faster and repair for free.
Ships 1 commandery wagon and 3 gatling guns. Allows training gatling guns in commanderies.

Belgian factory occupying population slots cannot produce artillery. Factory wagons are available at the town center. Each factory slows unit training, especially settlers, but speeds up building construction and age advancements (effects persist even if factories are destroyed).
Allows Sergeants to gain combat promotions, additionally increasing their range and attack damage for each enemy military unit killed. Sergeants now train slower.
Allows fishing boats to attack other ships and buildings
All fishing boats are sold for influence (50 per boat)
Military ship cost is split between influence and wood
Docks can fire at enemy ships without needing to garrison fishing boats.
Ships 150 food for each shipment sent. This technology's cost increases with each age.
Ships 125 wood for each shipment sent. This technology's cost increases with each age.
Ships 100 gold for each shipment sent. This technology's cost increases with each age.
Allows counter-jaegers to gain combat promotions, additionally increasing their range and attack damage for each enemy military unit killed.
Melee cavalry and assault infantry deal more damage to buildings. Mounted archers attack buildings with fire arrows that deal burning damage over time.
Ships 3 tavern wagons. Turns taverns into mercenary camps, which can be built in greater numbers. Replaces mercenary and bandit costs with influence equivalents.

Each time ships different units from the German royal house in the following order:
 • 13 black brunswickers
 • 8 mounted infantry
 • 5 Saxon cuirassiers
Weak but cheap musketeer, effective against cavalry.
Ships 1 fort wagon. Allows training allied units in forts.
Instead of food, peasants consume less wood. Training any unit grants wood instead of food for construction.

Arrives quickly! Advancing to the commerce age requires less food, and subsequent age-ups require less gold. Advancing to the next age ships 2 settler wagons. If this card is sent in later ages, additional settler wagons arrive.
All infantry train faster. Veteran infantry upgrades and arsenal infantry upgrades are researched.
All cavalry train faster. Veteran cavalry upgrades and arsenal cavalry upgrades are researched.
All artillery train faster. Arsenal artillery upgrades are researched, and the next artillery foundry upgrade becomes free.
(Team) 6 bogenschützen. Veteran upgrades for musketeers and their analogs become free (does not apply to local warrior, spear thrower, or tomahawk upgrades).
Ships 3 mountain soldiers for every 2 shipments sent during the game. Each subsequent shipment adds 1 mountain soldier.
Ships 1 mountain soldier for every 1 shipment sent during the game. Each subsequent shipment adds 1 mountain soldier.
Ships 2 mountain soldiers for every 1 shipment sent during the game. Each subsequent shipment adds 1 mountain soldier.
Ships 3 haiduks. Haiduks are available in taverns; all bandits occupy less population.
Culverins are more effective at destroying enemy buildings.
Ships 5 black riders and a tavern wagon. Black riders are available in taverns.

(Team) Ships 2 mountain soldiers. Mountain soldiers deal increased damage to guardians. Killing guardians grants more XP. (Penalties for damage against settlers, buildings, and heroes are nullified at the start of the commerce age)
Ships 3 heavy dragoons. Increases their training speed and allows training heavy dragoons in the commerce age.
Mountain soldiers and chevaulegers are available in unlimited numbers but now occupy population slots. All Wittelsbach upgrades are available in local embassies.
Ships 1 tavern wagon. Taverns start producing food and wood but reduce gold gathering.
Ships 2 tavern wagons. Taverns produce more resources.
Ships 8 jaegers and a tavern wagon. Jaegers are available in taverns.
Ships 6 giant grenadiers and a tavern wagon. Grenadiers are available in taverns.
Ships 3 landsknechts and a tavern wagon. Landsknechts are available in taverns.
Ships 9 Vyatka brigadiers and a tavern wagon. Brigadiers are available in taverns.
Ships 5 waffennmen and a tavern wagon. Waffennmen are available in taverns.
Ships 5 Hamelin grenadiers and a tavern wagon. Grenadiers are available in taverns.
Light cavalry deals increased melee damage, especially against heavy cavalry and assault infantry. Heavy cavalry deals increased damage against light infantry.
Increases XP gained from killing enemy units and destroying buildings by 33%.
Allows brewers to slowly gather food at mills and gives them a long-range attack; also allows training them at mills.
Ships several Russian musketeers and allows training them in forts. The barracks gains access to the recruit reformation.
Ships several Balkan skirmishers and allows training them in forts. The barracks gains access to the skirmisher reformation.
Ships several swordsmen and allows training them in forts. The barracks gains access to the swordsman reformation.
All existing pikemen become Russian recruits. Recruits are available for training in barracks and galleons; they become stronger with pikemen upgrades.
All existing brewers become Balkan skirmishers. Skirmishers are available for training in barracks and galleons; they become stronger with brewer upgrades.
All existing rondeliers become Macedonian swordsmen. Swordsmen are available for training in barracks and galleons; they become stronger with rondelier upgrades.
Ships an additional explorer and increases their attack damage. Explorers can no longer be redeemed via the town center.
Heavily armored mounted shooter, effective against infantry.
Mercenaries gain increased hit points and training speed at the start of each age (up to 20%).

Opportunistic contracts: mercenaries' hit points and training speed increased by 10%
Opportunistic contracts: mercenaries' hit points and training speed increased by 15%
Opportunistic contracts: mercenaries' hit points and training speed increased by 20%
Allows hiring mercenary infantry and cavalry in any age at a reduced cost.
Ships 1 capitol wagon. Gives your capitol unique upgrades.
Ships settlers on the first send and couriers on the second.
Coureurs des bois become available at estates. Allows training up to 30 coureurs beyond the normal villager limit.
Militia can gain combat promotions, additionally increasing their range and hit points for each enemy military unit killed. Each technology researched at the monastery grants free militia.
Takes a long time! After researching the technology, resources are returned in greater amounts.

Can be built over mines to produce coin. Slowly generates natural resources or war bears, and allows establishing relations with European states or locals to obtain their units and unique technologies.

Mounted infantry, survivalists, and Habsburg upgrades are available at the local embassy. Unit creation limit is doubled, but they now occupy population slots. Similar units from local settlements will also have an increased creation limit but will NOT occupy population slots. +1 wagon for the frontier factory.

Wittelsbach units and upgrades are available at the local embassy. Unit creation limit is doubled, but they now occupy population slots. Similar units from local settlements will also have an increased creation limit but will NOT occupy population slots. +1 wagon for the frontier factory.

Watchtowers repair for free and automatically, and can now be sold for 75% of the spent resources. Allows placing Grenzers on watchtowers. Units placed on a watchtower increase its damage by 10%. +1 wagon for the watchtower.
Watchtowers and Grenzers increase damage against guards; Grenzers can now collect treasures. All enemies and treasures are temporarily revealed on the map. +1 wagon for the watchtower.

Phanariot units and upgrades are available at the local embassy. Unit creation limit is doubled, but they now occupy population slots. Similar units from local settlements will also have an increased creation limit but will NOT occupy population slots. +1 wagon for the frontier factory.
