Bourbon units and upgrades are available at the local embassy. Unit creation limit is doubled, but they now occupy population slots. Similar units from local settlements will also have an increased creation limit but will NOT occupy population slots. +1 wagon for the frontier factory.
Belgian infantry significantly increases melee damage. Skirmishers are equipped with melee weapons that have high base damage but reduced multipliers.

Places the March Revolution banner, which boosts morale. Military units (except artillery) increase their attack range, while enemy units receive a range penalty. Consular units increase health and attack power under the banner. The banner cannot be placed near starting town centers. Only one banner can be placed at any time.
Sends 1 wagon for the town center and increases their construction limit. Town centers slowly produce all resources. Ordinance pikemen are available in commanderies, steamships, and forts.
Teutonic heavy infantryman, ready for any situation. Specializes in utilizing the strengths of various formations.
Each house supports additional population and creates 1 English archer. English archers are available in commanderies, steamships, and forts.
Instead of food, boats earn money when fishing. To cancel, the shipment can be sent again.
Mounted shooter, effective against heavy cavalry and artillery. Deals increased damage to ancient infantry.
Dragoons increase melee damage, especially against heavy cavalry and assault infantry. Reiters increase ranged damage, especially against artillery.
Churches are cheaper, researched and built faster, more durable, available earlier, limit increased, additional team experience increased.

Arrives quickly! Transition to the trade age requires less food, and transitions to subsequent ages require less gold.\n • With each age, Bavaria's allies receive additional transition speed to new ages (up to -50%), the effect does not apply to Bavaria itself.
Mercenaries have increased health and damage. All units except mercenaries and bandits take longer to create.
2 wagons for the tavern or church
2 wagons for the estate. Built mills create a settler wagon, similar to taverns.
Delivers 1 wagon for the church or tavern. Gives your church unique upgrades.
2 Mangudai and 1 rickshaw for the castle
4 Mangudai and 1 rickshaw for the castle
6 Mangudai and 1 rickshaw for the castle
The Tatar explorer's passive ability allows nearby units to gain twice as much experience.
Delivers an additional explorer and doubles the effectiveness of the experience-giving aura.
Increases the health of Keshiks, steppe riders, and Tarkhans. Grants a damage bonus when fighting in groups.\n • In the imperial age, bonuses increase
Delivers several iron flails and meteor hammers, increases their health and attack power. Allows them to protect nearby cavalry.
Sends 2 wagons for the hacienda and 1 battleship. Battleships are available in ports.\n • If the hacienda is lost, it can be rebuilt through the factory wagon.
The cost of transitioning to the III age will be reduced by -200 food and gold
Villages gather resources faster. Livestock gains weight faster and produces resources more efficiently in the village.
Maximum population limit increased to 230. Herd horse creation limit increased by 10.
Bandit units require less population. Wokou riders can be created in the tavern.
With each kill, health will decrease to 1.
Summon: call Akıncı to defend this city!
Summon: call Akıncı to defend this village!
Delivers several Tarkhans, increases their health, and allows them to periodically shoot flaming arrows.

Keshiks, steppe riders, and Tarkhans become Bashkirs (Tatar archers). Allows training and upgrading them, but they now occupy population slots. Allows selecting Bashkirs when transitioning to the III/IV ages.
Mounted shooter, effective against heavy cavalry and artillery, deals additional damage to ancient infantry.
Foot crossbowmen shoot farther and deal more damage to heavy infantry. Mounted crossbowmen deal more damage to heavy cavalry and shoot farther.
Swiss heavy infantry deals more damage to cavalry and assault infantry. Grants a health bonus when fighting in groups.\n • In the imperial age, bonuses increase
Allows Alpini and sappers to receive combat promotions, additionally increasing health and damage for each destroyed enemy military unit.
4 wagons for the house and 1 random mine
4 wagons for the estate and 1 random mine
Reduces the cost of all cavalry, especially Martolos. Cavalry upgrades are researched significantly faster.
Gives explorers access to the "Hot Air Balloon" ability\n\nKleft: status "Available" selected

Delivers several Macedonian skirmishers. At the start of a new age, this card is restored, and the number of delivered Klefts increases. In the imperial age, this card becomes completely infinite but sends fewer units than in the industrial age.
Macedonian light guerrilla with slow attacks. Effective against heavy infantry and light cavalry in melee, periodically throws a grenade. Protects nearby units.
Increases experience gained for killing enemy units and destroying buildings by 25%.
Strengthens the morale of nearby Belgian units. The flag must be defended—if enemies capture it, it will be removed from the map.

Placed by a general to strengthen nearby Belgian units. If there are no allied units or buildings nearby, the flag can be captured by the enemy and will be removed from the map.
 • Gives consular units +10% to damage and health.
 • Gives all infantry and cavalry +15% to attack range.
 • Gives enemy units -10% to attack range.
Sturdy German colonial houses provide housing for a larger population.
Light infantry have increased attack power and health.
Heavy infantry have increased attack power and health.
Sends 3 scout knights for each town center and trading post (up to 18).
Sends 1 scout knight for every 2 buildings (up to 20).
Sends 2 scout knights for each military camp (up to 9), + 1 scout knight.
Peasants gather wood 20% faster. You receive 10% of all gathered wood back (up to 1500 units of resources).
Peasants gather gold from mines 20% faster. You receive 10% of all gathered gold back (up to 1500 units of resources).
(Command) Delivers 3 regular pikemen. Allows allies to create Swiss pikemen in taverns, saloons, monasteries, or palaces. Local natives will also be able to create them in the local embassy.
The cost of foot crossbowmen, mounted crossbowmen, and pikemen is replaced with an equivalent amount of gold. Allows creating mounted crossbowmen in the trade age.
All crossbowmen are converted into skirmishers, and all pikemen are converted into halberdiers.

Scout knights deal more damage to light cavalry at a distance. Teutonic crossbowmen shoot farther and deal more damage to heavy infantry. All infantry with crossbows can attack buildings from afar with flaming bolts, which gradually deal burning damage.
Scout knights have increased attack power and health.
Peacekeeper knights have increased attack power and health.
You can call Stetzers to defend watchtowers.
Ships fire faster, Stetzers increase melee defense.
Powerful defensive building where Teutonic and local units are created. Units placed in it require less population. Holds 20 population units.
7 pigs and 1 wagon for the estate
Increases attack power and health of commanders and order units.

Teutonic Order commander, uses various passive auras—can increase movement speed, damage, health, experience gained, siege damage, as well as heal or absorb damage. Is a melee heavy infantry, effective against cavalry.

Delivers 11 royal arquebusiers. Allows hiring royal arquebusiers at the native embassy and vanguard.\n • Unit creation limit is doubled, but they now occupy population slots. Similar units from local settlements will also have an increased creation limit but will NOT occupy population slots.

Delivers 9 mountain soldiers. Allows hiring mountain soldiers at the native embassy and vanguard.\n • Unit creation limit is doubled, but they now occupy population slots. Similar units from local settlements will also have an increased creation limit but will NOT occupy population slots.

Delivers 7 hussars of death. Allows hiring mountain soldiers at the native embassy and vanguard.\n • Unit creation limit is doubled, but they now occupy population slots. Similar units from local settlements will also have an increased creation limit but will NOT occupy population slots.

Delivers 15 northern musketeers. Allows hiring northern musketeers at the native embassy and vanguard.\n • Unit creation limit is doubled, but they now occupy population slots. Similar units from local settlements will also have an increased creation limit but will NOT occupy population slots.

Delivers 14 line infantry. Allows hiring line infantry at the native embassy and vanguard.\n • Unit creation limit is doubled, but they now occupy population slots. Similar units from local settlements will also have an increased creation limit but will NOT occupy population slots.

Delivers 8 chevaulegers. Allows hiring chevaulegers at the native embassy and vanguard.\n • Unit creation limit is doubled, but they now occupy population slots. Similar units from local settlements will also have an increased creation limit but will NOT occupy population slots.
Delivers 7 winged hussars. Allows hiring winged hussars at the native embassy and vanguard. Now winged hussars occupy population slots but have no creation limit.

Delivers 8 dragoons. Allows hiring dragoons at the native embassy and vanguard.\n • Unit creation limit is doubled, but they now occupy population slots. Similar units from local settlements will also have an increased creation limit but will NOT occupy population slots.

Delivers 11 Saxon cuirassiers. Allows hiring Saxon cuirassiers at the native embassy and vanguard.\n • Unit creation limit is doubled, but they now occupy population slots. Similar units from local settlements will also have an increased creation limit but will NOT occupy population slots.
Ancient ranged shooter, effective against light infantry.
Order summon: call 10 archers for defense.
Order summon: call 10 halberdiers for defense.
Order summon: call 8 archers for defense.
Order summon: call 8 halberdiers for defense.
Order summon: call 8 crossbowman for defense.
Order summon: call 8 pikemen for defense.
Allows all infantry from the barracks (landwehr, schützen, musketeer, skirmisher) to receive combat promotions, additionally increasing health and attack range for each destroyed enemy military unit.

Allows all cavalry from the stable (uhlan, chevauleger, pistolier) to receive combat promotions, additionally increasing health and damage for each destroyed enemy military unit. Pistoliers receive more significant veteran promotions.
Upgrades for all military units cost less.
All your landwehr are converted into schützen.
All your landwehr and schützen are converted into skirmishers
This unit receives a promotion for each destroyed enemy military unit. Its health and attack radius increase.
Arrives quickly! All barracks, stables, and artillery yards are converted into military wagons.
With each shipment, 2 additional uhlans are delivered.
With each shipment, 3 additional landwehr are delivered.
You can build additional military buildings, which are now cheaper.
Converts into a border vanguard containing Teutonic units.
Allows building a capitol and researching most technologies in it without transitioning to the imperial age. Upgrading local warriors costs less.
Military buildings provide more additional units after construction. Prussian infantry and cavalry are created faster.

Fast heavily armed rider, effective against skirmishers and artillery. At the start of battle, performs a powerful pistol shot. Increases health when near natural resources. Has limited potential for upgrades
"Order summon" can be used more often.\n • Order summon: cooldown -40%
"Order summon" -50% cost, but now summons 2 fewer units.
Hunters become cheaper. Trees provide resources much longer.
Very fast transition to the next age, provides 1 shipment
+40% to consular unit attack power and health
-50% to mercenary attack power, health, and cost
2 hunters and wagon for the market
4 half-fed pigs and wagon for the estate

A fast, heavily armed cavalry unit effective against skirmishers, artillery, and buildings. Throws a grenade at the start of battle. Gains increased health when near natural resources. Has limited upgrade potential.
Arrives quickly! With each shipment starting from this one, 2 additional uhlans are included.
Arrives quickly! With each shipment starting from this one, 3 additional musketeers are included.
Arrives quickly! All barracks, stables, and artillery depots are converted into military wagons.

Military buildings NO longer provide additional units after construction. Military buildings become available in unlimited quantities and are cheaper, and can now be built by musketeers and grenadiers.
Allows the construction of a Capitol and the research of most technologies at a reduced cost without advancing to the Imperial Age. Upgrades for native warriors are significantly cheaper.
Upgrades for all military units are cheaper. Landwehr become more expensive, while other Prussian military units deal less damage to settlers.
Prussian Military: Reduced damage to peasants
A powerful defensive building where Teutonic and native warriors are trained. Units garrisoned inside require less population. Can hold 10 units.

Farms automatically collect resources from fattened livestock. Settlers gather resources from herdable animals faster. The creation limit for sheep and pigs is significantly increased, and they gain weight faster (especially pigs).

Ships 7 Winged Hussars. Allows the recruitment of Winged Hussars at the native Embassy and vanguard.\n • Unit creation limit is increased, but they now occupy population slots. Similar units from local settlements will also occupy population slots.

Ships 8 Drabants. Allows the recruitment of Drabants at the native Embassy and vanguard.\n • Unit creation limit is increased, but they now occupy population slots. Similar units from local settlements will also occupy population slots.

Ships 11 Saxon Cuirassiers. Allows the recruitment of Saxon Cuirassiers at the native Embassy and vanguard.\n • Unit creation limit is increased, but they now occupy population slots. Similar units from local settlements will also occupy population slots.

Ships 15 Northern Musketeers. Allows the recruitment of Northern Musketeers at the native Embassy and vanguard.\n • Unit creation limit is increased, but they now occupy population slots. Similar units from local settlements will also occupy population slots.

Ships 14 Line Infantry. Allows the recruitment of Line Infantry at the native Embassy and vanguard.\n • Unit creation limit is increased, but they now occupy population slots. Similar units from local settlements will also occupy population slots.

Ships 8 Chevau-légers. Allows the recruitment of Chevau-légers at the native Embassy and vanguard.\n • Unit creation limit is increased, but they now occupy population slots. Similar units from local settlements will also occupy population slots.

Ships 11 Royal Arquebusiers. Allows the recruitment of Royal Arquebusiers at the native Embassy and vanguard.\n • Unit creation limit is increased, but they now occupy population slots. Similar units from local settlements will also occupy population slots.

Ships 9 Mountain Troops. Allows the recruitment of Mountain Troops at the native Embassy and vanguard.\n • Unit creation limit is increased, but they now occupy population slots. Similar units from local settlements will also occupy population slots.

Ships 7 Death Hussars. Allows the recruitment of Mountain Troops at the native Embassy and vanguard.\n • Unit creation limit is increased, but they now occupy population slots. Similar units from local settlements will also occupy population slots.

Ships 14 Black Brunswickers. Allows the recruitment of Line Infantry at the native Embassy and vanguard.\n • Unit creation limit is increased, but they now occupy population slots. Similar units from local settlements will also occupy population slots.
Sends 2 vanguard wagons and allows them to be built earlier.
Sends 2 military wagons. All buildings are constructed slightly faster.
Allows cavalry from the stable (uhlans, chevau-légers, mounted grenadiers) to receive combat upgrades, additionally increasing their health and damage for each enemy military unit destroyed.
Allows the training of grenadiers. Equips grenadiers and klephts with hand mortars.
Ships 2 hussars for each military production building you own.
(Team) You receive a 500 wood crate for each ally and send each ally 1 town center wagon.
Increases church work speed, XP production, and build limit.
Farms, mills, and estates become cheaper; livestock gain weight faster.
1 manor house wagon and 200 coin crates
2 manor house wagon and 300 coin crates
3 manor house wagon and 400 coin crates
4 manor house wagon and 500 coin crates
4 Napoleonic cannons and access to them at the tavern
Ships adventurers and converts militia into them, allows their creation in galleons and privateers. Adventurers can now slowly gather resources from mines. Bandits occupy less population.
Provides and maintains an additional 20 population beyond the limit.
Allows the creation of a prospector wagon at the town center; mines provide resources for longer.
Blueberries increase unit health; just don't forget to eat them.
Slowly gathers nearby trees and produces influence. Influence production increases for each nearby tree.
Ships a Lumber Camp wagon and increases their build limit, increases the number of trees gathered simultaneously.
Ships a Lumber Camp wagon and increases their build limit by 2.
Ships 2 mounted militia for each military production building you own.
Reduces the cost of barracks and stables.
Units gain increased line of sight and movement speed.
Increases gathering speed at farms and estates.
Training settlers and constructing buildings is faster and grants influence instead of XP for construction.
Musketeers upgrade to Fencibles, which are stronger, cheaper, and have increased attack range. Militia are healed and become musketeers.
Militia are healed and become Fencible musketeers.
Métis Shooters upgrade to Volunteers, which are stronger, cheaper, and have increased attack range. Militia are healed and become Volunteers.
Militia are healed and become Volunteers.
Snipers upgrade to Forest Wardens, which are cheaper, increase attack range and health when near trees. Militia are healed and become Forest Wardens.
Militia are healed and become Forest Wardens.
Plants a tree near the Lumber Camp, which will be slowly gathered and produce influence.
Increased health and attack strength for mercenaries
Ships a group of musketeers. Converts militia into musketeers, healing them first.
Ships a group of Métis Shooters. Converts militia into Métis Shooters, healing them first.
Ships a group of dragoons. Converts mounted militia into dragoons, healing them first. Allows the creation of dragoons in the Commerce Age.
Ships a group of dragoons. Converts mounted militia into dragoons, healing them first.
Ships a group of hussars. Converts mounted militia into hussars, healing them first.
Ships a group of snipers. Converts militia into snipers, healing them first.

The market can be built for free, and an already built market refunds resources. Allows exchanging influence for gold and vice versa. The market provides a small gold trickle, and nearby units increase this trickle.
Heavily armed bandit cavalry. Effective against heavy infantry and light cavalry. Increases damage dealt when near natural resources.
Northern anti-skirmisher bandit. Effective against light infantry. Increases damage dealt when near natural resources.
Northern spear bandit. Effective against cavalry and buildings. Increases damage dealt when near natural resources.
More expensive than a regular house. Slowly produces coureur des bois after construction; compactly built manor houses accelerate each other's production. Supports 10 population.
Ships 1 pioneer and allows them to gather resources from nearby animals, berry bushes, mines, and resource crates.
Ships 1 Lumber Camp wagon and influence tokens equivalent to 300 influence. Increases the Lumber Camp build limit by 1.
Iroquois units and upgrades are available at the local Embassy. Unit creation limit is doubled, but they now occupy population slots. +1 frontier outpost wagon.
Militia can be trained at the town center and outpost. Militia train faster, but their cost is converted to equivalent influence. The shipment can be sent again to refund the cost.
Militia can be trained at houses, but their cost is converted to equivalent influence. The shipment can be sent again to refund the cost.
Chapels can now slowly create groups of foot militia for free, but militia become significantly weaker. Each technology researched at the chapel provides free additional militia. +1 chapel wagon.
Ships 800 wood crates. Now, with each shipment, all Lumber Camps create 1 tree.
Chapels create units faster, including free ones. Consulate units become stronger and are trained for 50% influence and 50% wood; this cost split can be canceled at the Lumber Camp.
Consulate units are trained only for influence.

Ships a militia officer who can receive shipments. Militia lose penalties to damage against settlers. Mounted militia attack more frequently. The Lumber Camp can craft specialized weapons for foot militia, allowing their damage multipliers to be changed.
Equips militia with rifled muskets, increasing their attack range and damage against infantry. Militia become vulnerable to heavy cavalry.
Equips militia with bayonet muskets, increasing their damage against cavalry and shock infantry. Militia become vulnerable to light infantry.
Equips militia with shotguns, increasing their splash damage and damage against mercenaries, natives, bandits, and buildings. Militia become vulnerable to spies and defensive buildings.
Light infantry deals increased damage to heavy infantry. Anti-skirmishers increase damage dealt to light infantry. Abus guns increase damage against all infantry.
All wood is converted into 50% influence and 50% coin.
Arrives slowly! Provides a small XP trickle for the rest of the game and restores the shipment. All enemies are temporarily revealed on the map.
(Team) Arrives slowly! Provides a small XP trickle for the rest of the game and 1 shipment to you and your allies. All enemies are temporarily revealed on the map.
All light cavalry and light infantry (including anti-skirmishers) train faster.
Ships an Arsenal wagon and allows all upgrades to be instantly researched. Anti-Infantry Volley becomes free.
Arrives quickly! Advancements to the III/IV/V Ages require wood instead of gold. Each new age ships an additional XP crate.
Each Lumber Camp creates 1 tree, and 1 elk is shipped for each Lumber Camp.
Ships 1 Lumber Camp builder and increases their build limit by 1. Each Lumber Camp creates 2 trees, and 2 elk are shipped for each Lumber Camp.
Ships a group of settlers and a covered wagon. The town center gains access to hiring Russian settlers, who are trained in groups for less food.
Ships 600 wood crates. Each Lumber Camp creates 2 trees.
Ships 900 wood crates. Each Lumber Camp creates 3 trees.
Ships 1300 wood crates. Each Lumber Camp creates 3 trees.
Ships 600 food crates. Each Lumber Camp creates 2 elk.
Ships 900 food crates. Each Lumber Camp creates 3 elk.
Ships crates with 1300 food. Each Lumber Camp creates 3 elk.
Ships chests with 600 gold and 1 mine.
Ships chests with 900 gold and 1 mine.
Ships chests with 1300 gold and 2 mines.
Ships a group of Danish Marines. Existing militia become Marines. Marines become available for hire via the Supply Wagon. All ships deal more damage to buildings.
Ships a group of Siberian Cossacks. Existing mounted militia become Cossacks.
Ships a group of Swedish Hussars. Existing mounted militia become Hussars.
Ships a group of Prussian Uhlans. Existing mounted militia become Uhlans.
Army of Russian nobles (cavalry archers) and culverin.\n5 cavalry archers: mounted archers, effective against cavalry.\n1 culverin: light artillery, effective against artillery.
Army consisting of Russian Oprichniki and culverins.\n9 Oprichniki: fast cavalry, effective for raiding villagers and buildings.\n3 culverins: light cannons effective against artillery.
Army of Swedish Caroleans.\n6 Caroleans: musketeers who fire quickly and counter cavalry at range, effective in melee.

Army of Swedish Life Guards (Hussars) and Caroleans.\n4 Life Guards: melee cavalry effective against ranged infantry, skirmishers and artillery.\n6 Caroleans: musketeers who fire quickly and counter cavalry at range, effective in melee.

Army of Swedish Dalkarls (pikemen) and infantry guns (leather cannons).\n15 Dalkarls: heavy infantry effective against cavalry and buildings.\n5 infantry guns: light cheap artillery that can move and fire quickly. Effective against infantry.
Sturdy Finnish horses increase the health of all cavalry, especially counter-dragoons.
Ships a group of snipers. Converts militia into snipers.
Danish light assault infantry that can fire at ships. Effective against heavy cavalry and artillery. Vulnerable to light infantry.

Bonus: +1 to military units' attack range

Trains: Caroleans, Hussars, Pikemen, Leather Cannons

Ships: Natural resources

Bonus: -10% to settler cost and training time

Trains: Cossacks, cavalry archers, Oprichniki, Culverins

Ships: Defensive buildings
Ships a large number of Caroleans and infantry guns.
Ships a group of settlers. Settlers gather natural resources faster.
Ships 6 Jaegers and upgrades all Jaegers to Savolak Jaegers. Savolak Jaegers have slightly better defense, can move stealthily, and cost partially wood to train.
Ships 8 Hakkapeliitta and upgrades them. Hakkapeliitta gain higher defense and cost partially wood to train.
Reduces cost of consulate armies and the consulate itself starts producing influence, but all native units now cost more resources. Each consulate technology adds a militia.
Ships 3 Tavern wagons. Converts Taverns into Mercenary Camps which can be built in greater numbers. Replaces mercenary and bandit costs with 50% influence and 50% wood.
Ships a group of Dutch Musketeers. Existing militia become Blue Coats. Blue Coats become available for hire via Supply Wagon. All musket infantry gain a damage bonus.
Ships a group of Spanish Rodeleros. Existing militia become Rodeleros. Rodeleros become available for hire via Supply Wagon. All heavy infantry gain a health bonus.
Ships a group of Danish Marines. Existing militia become Marines. Marines become available for hire via Supply Wagon. All ships deal more damage to buildings.
Ships a group of French Grenadiers. Existing militia become Grenadiers. Grenadiers become available for hire via Supply Wagon. All infantry gain a siege damage bonus.
Ships a group of Dutch Reiters. Existing mounted militia become Reiters. Reiters become available for hire via Supply Wagon. All light cavalry gain an attack range bonus.
Ships a group of French Gendarmes. Existing mounted militia become Gendarmes. Gendarmes become available for hire via Supply Wagon. All heavy cavalry gain a damage bonus.
Snipers gain increased attack damage and health; when near trees they gain increased attack range and health.
Balkan skirmisher, effective against heavy infantry and light cavalry. In melee combat, deals higher base damage due to lower additional damage.
Venetian bank producing resources of choice, settlers, or experience.
Supply Wagon — accelerates the gathering of natural resources by nearby settlers. Can build a house, market, merceria, church, port, factory, or pen.
Supply Wagon — accelerates the gathering of all resources by nearby settlers. Can build a mill, estate, town center, or capitol.
Supply Wagon — accelerates production in nearby military buildings. Can build an outpost, barracks, stable, tavern, arsenal, artillery yard, or local embassy.
Settler, but an experienced peasant who gathers resources quickly.

Venetian Sutler — a settler who excels at working in agricultural buildings but is less effective at gathering natural resources. Cannot construct buildings. Provides a gathering bonus to nearby Apprentices.
Venetian Apprentice — an inefficient settler. Cannot construct buildings. Receives a gathering bonus when near a Sutler.
Sends 3 Croats. Allows hiring Croats in taverns and stables at a reduced cost. All bandits occupy less population.
Sends 3 Bandits. Allows hiring Bandits in taverns and stables at a reduced cost. All bandits occupy less population.
Sends 3 Cossack Bandits. Allows using Cossack Bandits in taverns and stables. All bandits occupy less population.
The Sutler creation limit is increased, and all settlers gain increased health.
The Sutler creation limit is further increased, and all settlers deal more damage.
Delivers 2 Trade Wagons and increases the creation limit of Mercerias and town centers by 2.
Delivers a unit of mounted archers. Cavalry receives all possible arsenal upgrades.
Dalmatian heavy infantry, very effective against cavalry and buildings.
Cypriot siege infantry with siphons filled with Greek fire, very effective against other infantry.
Venetian heavy infantry that can fire at ships. Quite versatile, effective against heavy cavalry at a distance. Vulnerable to light infantry and artillery.

Shaolin units and upgrades are available at the local embassy. Unit creation limit is doubled, but they now occupy population slots. Similar units from local settlements will also have an increased creation limit but will NOT occupy population slots. +1 wagon for the border factory.
Delivers a group of archers, exceeding the creation limit. After hiring mercenaries of a similar unit type, all archer deliveries will be replaced by them.

Delivers a group of Fanti da Mar, exceeding the creation limit. After hiring mercenaries of a similar unit type, all Fanti da Mar deliveries will be replaced by them.\n • If mercenaries occupy more population, additional payment will be required, and if they occupy less population, deliveries will refund half their cost.
Delivers a group of sappers, exceeding the creation limit. After hiring mercenaries of a similar unit type, all sapper deliveries will be replaced by them.
Delivers a group of pikemen, exceeding the creation limit. After hiring mercenaries of a similar unit type, all pikemen deliveries will be replaced by them.
(Team) Mercenaries receive a slight improvement in attack strength and health.
Infantry and cavalry from the barracks and stable are available in greater numbers and at a reduced cost.
Mercenary deliveries become cheaper and arrive faster, BUT now with each delivery, mercenaries and bandits receive a bonus to attack strength instead of health.
Delivers a group of flamethrowers, exceeding the creation limit. After hiring mercenaries of a similar unit type, all flamethrower deliveries will be replaced by them.

Delivers a group of Palikars, exceeding the creation limit. After hiring mercenaries of a similar unit type, all Palikar deliveries will be replaced by them.\n • If mercenaries occupy more population, additional payment will be required, and if they occupy less population, deliveries will refund half their cost.

Delivers a group of carabineers, exceeding the creation limit. After hiring mercenaries of a similar unit type, all carabineer deliveries will be replaced by them.\n • If mercenaries occupy more population, additional payment will be required, and if they occupy less population, deliveries will refund half their cost.
Additional Banderias for Germans and Italians
All wagons become cheaper and move significantly faster.
Delivers a powerful battleship! Allows Venetian battleships to deal critical damage.
Supply Ship — accelerates the gathering of natural resources by nearby boats, can transport 5 units. Can build a port, factory, or outpost.
Order a Venetian Carrack at the standard cost.
Enemy ships receive passive damage even when outside the attack range.
Unit upgrades require only gold and are researched instantly.
Settlers gather natural resources and work in economic buildings 15% faster.
Delivers 1 dock wagon and improves their healing and ship creation speed, allows docks to fire without boats. Docks now automatically and freely create fishing boats.
Unique bonus for Italians: +5% to melee attack.
Unique bonus for Italians: +5% to ranged attack.
5 Fanti da Mar and 2 Military Wagons
1 Battleship, creation limit for all ships is increased
Unique bonus for Italians: +5% to health.
If we lose, we'll just say it was sabotage.
(Team) Delivers an additional Condottiero and allows him to build field hospitals for free. Inquisitors become available and can be trained in field hospitals.

Delivers an additional Condottiero, converts barracks and stables into military camps (Maltese hospital). Allows Condottiero to build military camps and mercenary camps. Military camps and mercenary camps can now heal nearby units.
Delivers mercenary Papal Bombards and increases area damage for all artillery.
Delivers an additional 1 Condottiero, and 1 more Condottiero for each era (up to 5). Condottieri now occupy population slots and can use Chaos on enemies.
Doge's infantry and cavalry increase their damage multipliers but take significantly longer to create.
Delivers a group of architects from neighboring republics who can construct buildings for gold.
Delivers a unit of Irish Brigadiers and allows their creation in mercenary tents. All Fanti da Mar deliveries are replaced by Irish Brigadiers.
Delivers a unit of Pandurs and allows their creation in mercenary tents. All Cretan Archer deliveries are replaced by Pandurs.
Delivers a unit of Giant Grenadiers and allows their creation in mercenary tents. All Fanti da Mar deliveries are replaced by Giant Grenadiers.
Delivers a unit of Highlanders and allows their creation in mercenary tents. All Fanti da Mar deliveries are replaced by Highlanders.
Delivers a unit of Savoyard Sharpshooters and allows their creation in mercenary tents. All Fanti da Mar deliveries are replaced by Savoyard Sharpshooters, but additional gold payment is required.
Delivers a unit of Fusiliers and allows their creation in mercenary tents. All Fanti da Mar deliveries are replaced by Fusiliers. Does NOT allow Fusilier creation in the II era.
Delivers a unit of Jaegers and allows their creation in mercenary tents. All Sapper deliveries are replaced by Jaegers.
Delivers a unit of Swiss Pikemen and allows their creation in mercenary tents. All Dalmatian Pikemen deliveries are replaced by Swiss Pikemen. Does NOT allow Swiss Pikemen creation in the II era.
Delivers a unit of Landsknechts and allows their creation in mercenary tents. All Dalmatian Pikemen deliveries are replaced by Landsknechts. Does NOT allow Landsknecht creation in the II era.
Delivers a unit of Mounted Sharpshooters and allows their creation in mercenary tents. All Palikar deliveries are replaced by Mounted Sharpshooters, but additional gold payment is required.
Delivers a unit of Mounted Arquebusiers and allows their creation in mercenary tents. All Palikar deliveries are replaced by Mounted Arquebusiers, but additional gold payment is required.
Delivers a unit of Elmeti and allows their creation in mercenary tents. All Palikar deliveries are replaced by Elmeti, but additional gold payment is required.

Delivers a unit of Stradiots and allows their creation in mercenary tents. All Palikar deliveries are replaced by Stradiots, but additional gold payment is required. Does NOT allow Stradiot creation in the II era.
Delivers a unit of Bosniaks and allows their creation in mercenary tents. All Palikar deliveries are replaced by Bosniaks, but additional gold payment is required.
Delivers a unit of Black Riders and allows their creation in mercenary tents. All Carabineer deliveries are replaced by Black Riders, but additional gold payment is required.
Delivers a unit of Royal Horsemen and allows their creation in mercenary tents. All Palikar deliveries are replaced by Royal Horsemen, but additional gold payment is required.
Farm and estate upgrades require only wood and are researched instantly.
Fishing boats and military ships gather food and earn money faster.
Peasants gather natural resources and butcher livestock faster. Herd animals gain weight faster.
Arrives quickly! Transitions to the next eras do not cost gold but require twice as much food and take much longer. Improves the food sale rate at the market by 50%.
Hussars, Rondeliers, and Halberdiers deal periodic bleeding damage.
Delivers several Hussars and Cuirassiers, reduces the cost of all cavalry upgrades, and speeds up their research.
Musketeers, Skirmishers, and Dragoons have a chance to deal critical damage when shooting.
(Team) Delivers a unit of Highlanders for the entire team.
Quick-learning Minuteman. Effective only when fighting in a group.
All castles gain access to free Skirmisher creation.
All castles gain access to free Pikeman creation.
All castles gain access to free Rondelier creation.
All castles gain access to free Musketeer creation.
All castles gain access to free Halberdier creation.
All castles gain access to free Hussar creation.
All castles gain access to free Dragoon creation.
All castles gain access to free Gendarme creation.
1 wagon for trading post and 5 skirmishers
1 wagon for trading post and 10 skirmishers
1 wagon for trading post and 15 skirmishers
+25% to attack strength and health of mercenaries and bandits
+25% to attack strength and health of mercenaries and bandits
Highlanders upgrade to Black Watch and become available in barracks. In the castle and town center, Highlanders can be summoned instead of militia.
Mercenaries' attack strength and health improve, BUT now their hiring requires food payment.
Allows hiring and upgrading bandits in the pub.
Upgrades the "Summon" ability to "Conscription." Conscription mobilizes more minutemen but is more expensive. Town centers deal more damage.
Increases the castle creation limit, and Highlander creation becomes available in the castle.
Farms automatically gather resources from fattened livestock. Cows become available on the farm, and selective improvement is researched for free.
Settlers gather resources from trees and berry bushes faster. Berry bushes become available in the town center.
Heavy infantry and cavalry gain increased health, light infantry and cavalry gain increased attack strength.
Settlers, infantry, and especially ranged infantry are created faster.
Sends an additional castle and increases their creation limit.
Grants access to unique MacKenzie clan shipments.
Improved Pub: pubs support population; pub creation limit increases.
(Team) Trade Empire: sends all allies a factory wagon; factories become cheaper and more durable.
Grants access to unique MacDonald clan shipments.
Peaceful Fishing: fishing boats and military ships gather food and earn money faster.
Privateers: delivers 2 privateer ships and allows their creation at the dock; sinking ships brings a small amount of gold.
Delivers 2 privateer ships and allows their creation at the dock; sinking ships brings a small amount of gold.
Grants access to unique Gordon clan shipments.
Improved Agricultural Technology: farm and estate upgrades require only wood and are researched instantly.
Distillery: gold income until the end of the game; estates become more efficient.
Grants access to unique MacLeod clan shipments.
Bagpipes: all units are created faster; units and buildings provide more experience when created.
Wallace's Defense Line: castles gain increased sight and attack range.
Grants access to unique MacPherson clan shipments.
Hard Times: transitions to the next eras do not require gold but cost twice as much food and take much longer; improves the food sale rate at the market by 50%.
Heather Feeding: farms automatically gather resources from fattened livestock; cows become available on the farm.
Grants access to unique Douglas clan shipments.
Flaming Blades: hussars, rondeliers, and halberdiers deal periodic bleeding damage.
Black Riders: delivers several hussars and cuirassiers, reduces the cost of all cavalry upgrades, and speeds up their research.
Grants access to unique Campbell clan shipments.
Argyle's Sight: dragoons gain increased attack range and sight range.
Grampian's Eye: musketeers, skirmishers, and dragoons have a chance to deal critical damage when shooting.
Grants access to unique Stuart clan shipments.
(Team) His Majesty's Highlanders: sends Highlanders to all allies.
Leather Mercenaries: mercenaries' attack strength and health improve; hiring mercenaries requires food payment.
Heather Fattening: Grain wagons passively generate resources, and can also receive economic shipments from the Home City and gather resources from crates.
Grain wagons passively generate resources, and can also receive economic shipments from the Home City and gather resources from crates.
Livestock farm wagon. Automatically produces sheep, generates a small amount of resources, and can also gather resources from nearby herdable animals.
All infantry and cavalry become slightly stronger. Melee infantry and cavalry gain additional hit points, while ranged infantry and cavalry increase their damage output.
All existing Town Centers produce 10 of Wallace's Militia.
Provides 1 Gold Prospector wagon. Mines last longer. All unit upgrades cost less gold.
Musketeers, Skirmishers, and Militia deal periodic poison damage when firing.
Stockpile with 800 wood; Crofts produce more wood.
(Team) Royal Dynasty: Trade route upgrades become free and available in any age for the entire team.
Allows grain wagons to gather resources from two animals simultaneously.
Allows grain wagons to gather resources from five animals simultaneously.
Arrives quickly! One randomly selected clan receives +15% to health pool and attack damage for its associated unit. Switching to this clan is 30% faster (if not already selected).
Pikemen improved by 15%, switching to MacDonalds is 30% faster
Musketeers improved by 15%, switching to Gordons is 30% faster
Skirmishers improved by 15%, switching to MacKenzies is 30% faster
Halberdiers improved by 15%, switching to MacPhersons is 30% faster
Clinkerne improved by 15%, switching to MacLeods is 30% faster
Hussars improved by 15%, switching to Douglases is 30% faster
Dragoons improved by 15%, switching to Campbells is 30% faster
Cuirassiers improved by 15%, switching to Stuarts is 30% faster
Hussars, Clinkernes, and Halberdiers deal periodic bleeding damage.
Flaming Blades: Hussars, Clinkernes, and Halberdiers deal periodic bleeding damage.
All castles gain access to free Clinkerne production.
Supports 25 population units and produces a small amount of wood.
(Team) Provides a small group of Highlanders. Allows allies to train Highlanders in Taverns, Saloons, Monasteries, or Palaces. Native Americans can also train them at their local Embassy.
Manors provide a small trickle of coin, and settlers work on them more efficiently.
Whiskey Distillery: Manors provide a small trickle of coin, and settlers work on them more efficiently.

Two types of settlers are available - Sutlers improve the efficiency of nearby Apprentice; Apprentice can be periodically summoned from the Town center. Settlers cannot construct buildings - supply wagons are used for construction and gathering upgrades. The unique version of the Lombard periodically produces resources of your choice or creates Sutlers. All military units have limited availability. The Home City offers mercenary shipments that make them available in Mercenary Camps.

Start the game with a Grain Wagon (similar to a Livestock Pen) that periodically produces sheep. Houses provide more population and passively generate wood. All units are slightly more weaker, expensive and train slower. Highlanders and Mounted Arquebusiers are always available at the Saloon (Pub). When advancing ages, you must choose a clan, which grants access to their unique shipments and allows Castles to train their associated unit for free.
Heather Feeding: grain wagons automatically create sheep and can also receive economic shipments from the Home City and gather resources from crates.
Grain wagons automatically create sheep and can also receive economic shipments from the Home City and gather resources from crates.
Scottish Royal Guard: Increases the castle creation limit, and Highlander training becomes available in the castle.
Dismantles the castle, refunding you the resources.
Delivers an additional 1 Condottiero, plus 1 more Condottiero for each era (up to 5). Condottieri now occupy population slots and can deal critical damage while taking minor damage themselves.
Military units train faster, especially mercenaries, +1 Condottiero
Churches are cheaper and more durable, and their limit is increased. Additional team experience is boosted, and constructing churches grants more experience.
Delivers 7 Vyatka Brigadiers and a tavern wagon. Brigadiers are available in taverns.
Livestock pen wagon. Produces a small amount of resources and can also gather resources from nearby herd animals.
Fulling Mills: peasants gather food much faster by butchering livestock.
The longer you wait, the more sheep will appear (up to 20).
Dragoons increase melee damage, especially against heavy cavalry and assault infantry. Reiters increase ranged damage, especially against artillery.
All fishing grounds and whales become visible for a short time.
Patrol: send a group of Highlanders!
Highlanders upgrade to Black Watch and become available in barracks. The town center can periodically summon Highlanders.
Creates Wallace's Militia—the longer you wait, the more (up to 40)—and temporarily increases castle firing speed.
The "Fish Farming" effect will end in
Imperial upgrade for frigates and monitors.
Castle rapid fire will end in
Sends 2 militia for each castle and allows summoning militia groups from castles. Castles become more durable and deal more damage.
Arrives quickly! All settlers become random infantry, or for every 2/3 settlers, a cavalry unit is sent.
Sends 1 castle and increases their creation limit.
All home wagons are removed, and in their place, additional grain wagons can now be created.
A home road that, after construction, creates a wagon for another such house.
(Team) Walls become more durable, and their upgrades require fewer resources.
MacDonald Representative: Pikemen receive a +10% bonus to health and attack strength; in IV, an additional +10% can be obtained through a castle technology.
MacLeod Representative: Clinkers receive a +10% bonus to health and attack strength; in IV, an additional +10% can be obtained through a castle technology.
Gordon Representative: Musketeers receive a +10% bonus to health and attack strength; in IV, an additional +10% can be obtained through a castle technology.
MacPherson Representative: Halberdiers receive a +10% bonus to health and attack strength; in IV, an additional +10% can be obtained through a castle technology.
Douglas Representative: Hussars receive a +10% bonus to health and attack strength; in IV, an additional +10% can be obtained through a castle technology.
Campbell Representative: Dragoons receive a +10% bonus to health and attack strength; in IV, an additional +10% can be obtained through a castle technology.
Stuart Representative: Cuirassiers receive a +10% bonus to health and attack strength; in IV, an additional +10% can be obtained through a castle technology.
Temporary settler, doesn't occupy population. Can only gather natural resources, exists for 1 minute. Duration increases by +1 minute with each age advancement.
Delivers 2 Badia plus 2 more Badia for every 5 minutes of game time (up to 30 minutes max).
Delivers 3 Badia plus 3 more Badia for every 5 minutes of game time (up to 30 minutes max).
Delivers 5 Badia plus 5 more Badia for every 5 minutes of game time (up to 30 minutes max).
Increases attack power of swordsmen, allows your fencers to gain combat promotions, additionally increasing health for each enemy military unit killed.
Assault infantry, effective against heavy infantry and light cavalry. Armed with single-shot pistols.
Supports 16 population units and gathers nearby resources.

Tatar Yurt. Functions as house, pen and stable. Supports 16 population units and gathers nearby resources. Can train and upgrade early cavalry, as well as breed livestock. Produces a herd horse after construction.
Allows maintaining 100 population units without yurts. Dance ground gains a ritual to increase max population limit.
Delivers several powerful musketeers and allows training them at local embassy. Janissaries gain reloadable pistol shot dealing siege damage.
Delivers several Siberian Cossacks and allows training them at local embassy.
Delivers several Russian Dragoons and allows training them at local embassy.
Delivers several Manchus and allows training them at local embassy. Manchus gain health regeneration when idle.
Delivers several Iron Warriors and allows training them at local embassy. Iron Warriors increase attack range (especially siege attack) and deal bonus damage to light cavalry.
Delivers several Azaps and allows training them at local embassy.
Tatar Explorer gains stealth movement and evasion.
Tatar Explorer gains ability to heal nearby units.
Consular units become stronger and train faster.
Delivers 1 Mosque Wagon. Allows researching all basic church upgrades, plus hiring Imams and Spies.
Delivers 1 Monastery Wagon. Allows researching unique upgrades, plus hiring Disciples and Wokou Penitents.
Delivers 1 Shrine Wagon. Allows researching local Tengri upgrades, plus hiring Shamans and Bashkir Archers.
Delivers 3 Tavern Wagons. Bandits upgrade and now consume less population. All allied shipment units become available in taverns.
Summons 1 Saigak per yurt plus 1 more Saigak per 5 minutes of game time (up to 30 minutes max).
Slowly arriving food shipment. Settlers gather food from natural sources faster.
Slowly arriving wood shipment. Settlers gather trees faster.
Slowly arriving coin shipment. Settlers gather gold from natural sources faster.
For next age: Settler cost -50%\nExtreme effect: Yurts stop gathering resources for 5 minutes
For next age: Food gathering +50%\nExtreme effect: Military unit training and building construction slowed by 100% for 5 minutes.
For next age: Wood gathering +40% and siege damage +30%\nExtreme effect: All units have 25% less health for 5 minutes.
For next age: Military unit training +40% and unit speed +10%\nExtreme effect: Settlers and animals train 100% slower for 5 minutes.
For next age: Settlers train for free automatically\nExtreme effect: Natural resource gathering slowed by 50% for 5 minutes
All yurts, stables, castles and outposts convert to builder wagons for relocation. Each subsequent 'Migration' takes longer and costs more.
Other 'Syncretism' shipments arrive instantly. Passive XP generation increases.
All buildings become more durable, walls become available earlier.
Livestock weight gain limit increases. Yurt cavalry costs less food to train.
All cavalry receive arsenal upgrades - heavy cavalry gains health and siege damage, light cavalry gains range and siege attack speed.
Fire Junks can be trained at docks. Dock heals faster and can fire without garrison.
Mercenaries and consular units receive significant attack and health bonuses.
Perform Mangudai ritual to receive mighty Mangudai horsemen.
Your peasants perform Mangudai ritual to receive mighty Mangudai horsemen.\nCurrent benefit:\n{danceDescription}
Perform Market Relations ritual to gradually receive gold.
Your peasants perform Market Relations ritual to gradually receive gold.\nCurrent benefit:\n{danceDescription}
Allows performing Market Relations ritual for gradual gold income.
Heavy cavalry that grows stronger with numbers. Effective against infantry and artillery. Can hide.
Scotland Forever: Killing units grants more XP, and each unit killed slightly reduces the cost to advance to the next age.
Killing units grants more XP, and each unit killed slightly reduces the cost to advance to the next age.
Hire 1 Sutler outside of settler training queue
All military units, especially Prussian infantry and cavalry, train faster.
Allows investing resources for conversion and researching bonuses received upon aging up.
Hire an army of Balkan mercenaries
(Team) Crossbowmen and pavise warriors shoot farther and prioritize attacking units they are effective against.
(Team) Training mercenaries grants double XP, and the enemy gains no XP for killing Swiss and allied mercenaries. Killing enemy mercenaries now grants gold instead of XP.

(Team) Allows the Swiss to train Swiss Pikemen at the Town Center. Allies gain access to training Swiss Pikemen at Taverns, Saloons, Monasteries, and Palaces, and local nations at the Embassy. +5 Swiss Pikemen
Arrives slowly! In exchange for gold, sends crates with an equal amount of food and wood. The cost and amount of resources increase with ages.
Arrives slowly! A shipment of 400 food and wood.
An irregular Swiss soldier serving as a musketeer, effective against cavalry. Can construct some buildings, gather treasures, collect resources from crates, and mine ore.
Sends an additional Swiss Pikeman with each shipment containing mercenaries. All shipments with regular Pikemen are replaced and now send Swiss Pikemen, but they arrive significantly slower.
Ships a group of Grenadiers and Cuirassiers. Grenadiers, Cuirassiers, and Dragoons are available in limited quantities from the Town Center.
Increases the radius of the Town Center's gold gathering bonus. Allows building one additional Town Center.
Unit upgrades do not require wood or food and are researched instantly.
Increases the range and attack speed of melee infantry units. Pikemen deal bonus damage to infantry, and Halberdiers and Swiss Pikemen deal bonus damage to heavy infantry.
Allows the Explorer to build Forts and Outposts. Alpini become available at the Fort in limited numbers.
Versatile infantry, protects nearby allies by absorbing a portion of damage dealt to them. Can build Forts and Outposts.
Increases the damage of Crossbowmen, Pikemen, Halberdiers, and Swiss Pikemen. Grants a health bonus when fighting in groups.\n • Bonuses increase in the Imperial Age
Pikemen receive penalties to their stats, but near a Town Center (including allied ones) their attack strength and health significantly increase.
Pikemen stats now increase near Town Centers, Outposts, and Forts (including allied ones).
Unique Swiss Capitol. Contains upgrades for automatically generated settlers and military unit upgrades.
Versatile infantry, protects nearby allies by absorbing a portion of damage dealt to them. Available from the Town Center.
Restores the barter function to the Market, but the cost of buying resources becomes more expensive.
Pikemen stats now increase near enemy Town Centers, Outposts, and Forts.
Ships 4 Outpost and Tavern Wagons. Increases the build limit for Outposts and Taverns.
Increases the Outpost build limit, all your buildings are repaired for 1000 hit points.
Ships a Coureur des Bois, allows training them at the local Embassy. Blocks access to the final settler limit upgrade.
Ships Settler Wagons, allows training them at the local Embassy. Blocks access to the final settler limit upgrade.
Ships an Architect, allows training them at the local Embassy. Blocks access to the final settler limit upgrade.
All settlers become Volkspikeniere, which do not cost population. Blocks access to the final settler limit upgrade.
Light assault infantry, effective against cavalry and artillery. Can receive promotions that improve damage dealt. Does not cost population.
Light infantry with high hit points but low damage multipliers.
Light ranged infantry effective against heavy infantry and light cavalry in melee, periodically throws a grenade. Protects nearby units.
Heavy ranged infantry effective against cavalry in melee, periodically throws a grenade. Protects nearby units.
Inefficient Congolese settler. Cannot construct buildings. May defect to the enemy or ally if no friendly units or buildings are nearby.
Each Badia becomes a Portuguese Cacador. Building construction cost reduced.
Heals units that have taken damage, but can also fight. Effective against heavy infantry and light cavalry. Does not grant XP when trained, instead grants it upon death.
Ships a Tower Builder and upgrades towers one age earlier.
Each Badia becomes a Portuguese Pikeman. Building construction cost reduced.
Each Badia becomes a Portuguese Crossbowman. Building construction cost reduced.
Each Badia becomes a Portuguese Musketeer. Building construction cost reduced.
Priests increase the area of effect of attacks, and all other units increase melee attack speed.
Converts into a Bazaar for resource conversion.
Adds one semi-fed Cow to all your future shipments.
Significantly improves the combat stats of Ombianga, but also increases their cost. Allows the Explorer to train a small group of Sekelulu Ombianga that do not cost population.
Increases the health and line of sight of ranged units, throwing units gain additional attack range.
Invests 500 coin into Bazaars, with each future shipment 100 coin is invested into the Bazaar.
Bazaar Builder and crates with 300 coin
Ships 15 influence for each enemy unit killed (up to 1500 resource value).
Ships 15 influence for each unit lost (up to 1500 resource value).
Ships 10 influence and 10 coin for each unit lost (up to 2000 resource value).
Ships 10 influence and 10 coin for each enemy unit killed (up to 2000 resource value).
Unit health slowly recovers — even in combat.
Available at Palaces: Dahomey Amazons, Askari, Kanuri, Sinnar Rider.
Adds additional resource production to Bazaars when converting investment resources.
Each Bazaar creates 1 Military Camp Builder.
Allows Mbunza Shooters and Ngola Grenadiers to receive combat promotions, additionally increasing their hit points and damage for each enemy military unit destroyed.
Field Repairs: Unit health slowly recovers — even in combat.
Treaty with Yavu: Available at Palaces: Dahomey Amazons, Askari, Kanuri, Sinnar Rider.
Mercenary Path: Dahomey Amazons, Askari, Kanuri, and Sinnar Riders increase attack strength and hit points by +50%
Allows researching unique Congolese upgrades at the Bazaar.
Fulbe White Cows: Adds one semi-fed Cow to all your future shipments.
Suya Kebabs: Villagers gather food faster from hunting and livestock.
Allows researching unique Yaka upgrades at the Bazaar.
Yaka Bazaars: Adds additional resource production to Bazaars when converting investment resources.
Military Investments: Each Bazaar creates 1 Military Camp Builder.
Allows researching unique Songye upgrades at the Bazaar.
Peaceful Fishing: Warships gather food and coin faster.
Slave Market: Increases the Badia build limit.
Underground Galleries: Invests 500 coin into Bazaars, with each future shipment 100 coin is invested into the Bazaar.
Improved Bazaars: Bazaars convert resources faster.
Allows training Copper Mine Builders at the Town Center.
Allows researching unique Lunda upgrades at the Bazaar.
Allows training Ombianga (Tomahawk Warrior) at the Palace.
Ngola Mbimbi: Increases the health and LoS of ranged units, throwing units gain additional attack range.
Ombianga Axes: Significantly improves the combat stats of Ombianga, but also increases their cost. Allows the Explorer to train a small group of Sekelulu Ombianga that do not cost population.
Allows researching unique Ovimbundu upgrades at the Bazaar.
Allows researching local Yoruba upgrades at the Bazaar.
Ships 1 Bazaar Builder, with each new age each Bazaar creates 2 Badia settlers.
Allows researching unique Jesuit upgrades at the Bazaar.
Supports 15 population and creates militia. Increases work rate on nearby farms, and attracts huntable animals.
Farm, Barn, and Market upgrades become free, but research much slower. Research time increases with each age.
Passive influence generation. Each living Badia increases generation by +0.05<icon="(32)(resources/images/hud/resource_influence.png)">.
The effect of 'Whisper of Chains' will end in
Temporarily reveals the LoS of enemy settlers.
Badia disappear from the map. For every 5 Badia, ships 4 Settlers.
Badia disappear from the map. For every 4 Badia, ships a crate of 400 influence.
Badia disappear from the map. For every 2 Badia, ships 1 Pikeman and 1 Archer.
Allows Nzambi Archers to damage buildings from a long distance. Significantly improves the siege damage of Kimbundu, Mpanza, and Kilombo.
Unlocks Cows at the Livestock Market.
Allows researching unique Fulbe upgrades at the Bazaar.
Ships 1 Grenadier for every 2 Badia. Badia disappear from the map. Resource gathering rate increases.
Ships 1 Dragoon for every 2 Badia. Badia disappear from the map. Resource gathering rate increases.
Ships 1 Skirmisher for every Badia. Badia disappear from the map. Resource gathering rate increases.
Ships 1 Gendarme for every 3 Badia. Badia disappear from the map. Resource gathering rate increases.
Increases the passive influence generation of the Explorer Ndambi.
Badia disappear from the map. For each Badia, sends 100 influence directly to the inventory.
Hanseatic Worker. As effective as four regular settlers, supports population for its own upkeep.
Unique Hansa penalty of -10% to attack strength and hit points
(Team) Increases attack strength and health of allied units. Hansa negates this bonus.
Landsknechts hired via the '%s' Trading Post!
Hires a squad of Landsknechts and allows them to be trained an age earlier and slightly faster.
Sends 1 Trading Post wagon, each Trading Post provides gold for its construction.
Building construction now costs 50% wood and 40% gold. Buildings that originally require gold (Fort) only split the wood cost.
Early light ranged cavalry, fights decently in melee. Effective against heavy cavalry and artillery.
Provides 1 Market wagon. Each Market creates 2 settlers after construction, but their number is now limited. The resource exchange rate at the Market becomes more favorable.
Reliable source of gold. Accumulates additional gold over time.
Sends 2 Bill of Exchange shipments, which gradually provide you with gold. You can have up to 10 Bill of Exchange shipments simultaneously.
Increases the dock's hit points, as well as ship repair and construction speed.
Significantly increases the attack range and broadside damage of Caravels.
Allows the Explorer to build Outposts and an additional early Fort. All defensive buildings cost less and are built faster.
All your buildings repair 1000 hit points.
Sends 3 Fluyt and increases the hit points of all warships.
Infantry costs less gold to train, infantry can be trained in batches of 10.
Sends 2 Horse Artillery battalions. Allows training Horse Artillery and Mortars an age earlier.
Infantry speed, damage, and health increased at a higher cost.
Farm, plantation, market, native settlement, and some unit upgrades no longer require food to research.
Sends 1 Bank wagon. The Explorer can rebuild the Bank, and upon death can rebuild itself at the Bank.
Mercenaries and their shipments are created and arrive faster.
Provides 1 Landsknecht for each Trading Post, Outpost, Fort, and Town Hall you have. Hiring Landsknechts, similar to Trading Posts, becomes available in Forts, Outposts, and Town Halls.
Regular settlers become cheaper and gather food from farms faster.
(Team) Provides books containing 500 XP. Increases the line of sight of all units and buildings.
Freiters deal more damage to settlers and trading posts. Freiters near trading posts gain a defense bonus.
Settlers gather resources from herdable animals faster. Sheep are replaced by pigs, which have a higher train limit and fatten faster.
Settlers and infantry cost less food to train. +5 Settlers
Mobilization! Peasants become Musketeers. "Desperation" can be reused twice, but with each mobilization the Musketeers become weaker.
Negation of the first mobilization penalty
Provides a powerful Ship of the Line! Ships of the Line now move faster and deal damage to all nearby units when exploding. Also, Fire Ships become available at the Dock.
Increases the movement speed of all your ships, especially Caravels and Fishing Boats. All warships except Ships of the Line can hold 25 more units.
Landsknechts, Jägers, Black Riders, and Giant Grenadiers are available for hire in the Tavern, Native Embassy, and via the Mercenary Banner.
Sends an additional Town Hall. All Trading Posts begin producing resources.
Improves the attack of warships and marine infantry.
Improves the attack and hit points of warships and marine infantry.
Improves the attack and hit points of warships and marine infantry. +3 Fluyt
Improves the building's attack against ships.
Provides 1 Salt Mine. Losing your own and killing enemy settlers refunds part of their cost.
Fishing Boats and Caravels gather gold faster. Caravels passively produce a small amount of gold.
Allows your Caravels, Fluyt, Frigates, and Marine Infantry to gain combat upgrades, additionally increasing damage and attack range for each enemy military unit destroyed.
(Team) Sends a postal wagon, improves Outposts and their equivalents.

(Team) Allows the Hansa to train Landsknechts in Barracks. Allies gain access to training Landsknechts in Taverns, Saloons, Monasteries, and Palaces, and native nations in the Embassy. +5 Landsknechts
Provides 1 Marine Infantry for each Dock and warship.
Native warriors become cheaper and available in greater numbers. If there is an allied native settlement, it sends a small squad of its units.
"March of the Knechts" will expire in
Increases the speed of all Knechts and prevents enemies from slowing them. Damage dealt during the march has no multipliers.
Killing enemy military units gives a small amount of gold, and an increased amount for mercenaries. The Mercenary Banner recovers 60 seconds faster and is available an age earlier.
The Mercenary Banner generates passive XP, each mercenary increases XP generation. Increases XP for killing units while the Mercenary Banner exists.
Hanseatic Captain's passive ability increases attack strength and hit points of nearby melee infantry by +10%.

Hanseatic Captain's passive ability increases attack strength and hit points of nearby melee infantry by +20%. The Hanseatic Captain increases its own attack strength and hit points for each nearby melee infantry and mercenary.
Improves the Hanseatic Captain's aura by 10% and enhances his combat abilities, additionally strengthening for each unit under his aura.
The Hanseatic Captain places a Mercenary Banner near himself. The Mercenary Banner allows training mercenaries from the Tavern and receiving shipments. Only one Banner can be placed at any time.
Allows training mercenaries from the Tavern and receiving shipments. If there are no allied units or buildings nearby, the Banner can be captured by the enemy and will be removed from the map.
Sends 2 Barracks wagons. Barracks support 5 population.
Sends 1 Covered Wagon, BUT does not increase the Town Hall train limit. Town Halls support an additional 5 population.
Town Halls passively produce food. Town Halls increase the train limit and support an additional 5 population.
Town Halls passively produce wood. Town Halls increase the train limit and support an additional 5 population.
Town Halls passively produce coin. Town Halls increase the train limit and support an additional 5 population.

Settlers gather wood from all sources faster. Town Halls increase the train limit and support an additional 5 population.
Settlers gather coin from all sources faster. Town Halls increase the train limit and support an additional 5 population.
(Team) Transition to the V Age is cheaper and researched faster.
Landsknechts occupy more population but benefit from upgrades aimed at bandits.
