Ranged shock infantry, bola thrower. Deals area damage, effective against heavy cavalry and artillery.
Light ranged infantry with a blowgun. Deals low damage compensated by a strong poison, effective against heavy infantry and light cavalry.
+50% to mercenary attack strength and hit points\n<color=0.9, 0.8, 0.5>Time:</color>\n70 <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_time.png)">
Blowgun Warrior deals more damage to heavy infantry, and its poison deals more damage to infantry.
Blowgun Warrior increases range, and its poison now lasts longer.
Blowgun Warrior increases movement speed, and its poison deals more damage to cavalry.
Blowgun Warrior increases rate of fire, and its poison deals damage more frequently.
Sends 2 Consulate builders and allows earlier training of European units.
Mercenary heavily armored sharpshooter with piercing damage, effective against most units. Vulnerable to light infantry and artillery.

Vasa units and upgrades are available in the Native Embassy. Unit train limit is doubled, but they now occupy population slots. Similar units from Native Settlements will also have an increased train limit, but will NOT occupy population slots. +1 Frontier Factory wagon.
All settlers, pikemen, and halberdiers become Papal Guards. Blocks access to the final settler limit upgrade.
Courland heavy rifle infantry, effective against cavalry in melee combat. Has low health and increased armor. Uses different types of defense depending on formation.
Courland settler from Mitava. Receives a bonus to resource gathering and construction speed around the Mitava Magistrate.
Courland town center. Allows training of Settlers from Mitava and improves their gathering around the center. Allows advancement to the II Age. Supports 10 population units.
Courland settler from Liepāja. Receives a bonus to resource gathering and construction speed around the Liepāja Magistrate.
Courland town center. Allows training of Settlers from Liepāja and improves their gathering around the center. Allows advancement to the III Age. Supports 25 population units.
Courland settler from Ventspils. Receives a bonus to resource gathering and construction speed around the Ventspils Magistrate.
Courland town center. Allows training of Settlers from Ventspils and improves their gathering around the center. Allows advancement to the IV Age. Supports 25 population units.
Courland settler from Kuldīga. Receives a bonus to resource gathering and construction speed around the Kuldīga Magistrate.
Courland town center. Allows training of Settlers from Kuldīga and improves their gathering around the center. Allows advancement to the V Age. Supports 25 population units.
Courland settler from Tukums. Receives a bonus to resource gathering and construction speed around the Tukums Magistrate.
Courland town center. Allows training of Settlers from Tukums and improves their gathering around the center. Supports 25 population units.

Advancing to the next Age is done via a shipment from the Home City. Upon completion of the advancement, an Covered Wagon will arrive from the town center.\n • The Town Center can continue to train settlers.
(Team) 1 Gold Mine wagon. Mines are marked on the map, military and economic upgrades receive a small discount.
Arrives quickly! Sends Burghers from Tukums above the population limit.
Ships an army of African mercenaries: Dahomey Amazons, Askari, and Kanuri Guards.
Light rifle infantry with a short-barreled musketoon, effective against heavy infantry and light cavalry. Deals high damage at medium range, and area damage at long range.
Versatile Courland rifle infantry, available in limited numbers. In a group, they increase attack strength and health.
Ships 3 Grenadiers and allows training them in Barracks.

Bonuses to resource gathering depending on settler type:
 • Mitava: +10% to all resources
 • Liepāja: +25% to food gathering
 • Ventspils: +25% to wood gathering
 • Kuldīga: +25% to gold gathering
 • Tukums: +20% to all resources
Infinite slow source of coin and passive export. Each worker at the mine increases passive export. A new Amber Mine becomes available each Age.

Increases the attack range of Minutemen. Skirmishers can now be created in the Commerce Age.\n • Strelets, Minutemen, and Skirmishers gain Amber Guard status and now receive stat bonuses near Amber Guardsmen.
(Team) Increases the training limit for settlers and their analogs.
Amber Guardsmen now increase defense when in a group.\n • The effect applies to all infantry with Amber Guard status.

Strelets receive a colossal boost to stats and range, but now take more population and part of their cost is paid with export.\n • Strelets gain Amber Guard status and now receive stat bonuses near Amber Guardsmen.
Infantry and cavalry are upgraded to Guards.
Mercenaries and Amber Guardsmen receive a stat boost.\n • Mercenaries gain Amber Guard status and now receive stat bonuses near Amber Guardsmen.
Mercenaries become slightly cheaper and stronger, and are available one Age earlier. Taverns become slightly cheaper and available in greater numbers.
Outlaws take less population. Taverns become slightly cheaper and available in greater numbers.\n • Outlaws gain Amber Guard status and now receive stat bonuses near Amber Guardsmen.
Musketeer infantry receives a stat bonus, specifically Šautinieks shoot farther and faster.\n • All Musketeers gain Amber Guard status and now receive stat bonuses near Amber Guardsmen.
Trading Posts become cheaper. Native Warriors are available in greater numbers but have less health.\n • Native Warriors gain Amber Guard status and now receive stat bonuses near Amber Guardsmen.
Amber Guardsmen can build Outposts, Barracks, Stables, Artillery Foundries, and Walls.\n • Buildings gain Amber Guard status and now receive stat bonuses near Amber Guardsmen.
Upgrades the 'Call to Arms' ability to 'Conscription'. Conscription mobilizes more Minutemen but costs more. Town centers deal more damage.
Sends 25 resources for each deceased Amber Guardsman. Now, each future shipment includes an additional Amber Guardsman and they now take less population.
Ships 500 coin. Improves the market buy rate by 20%.
Vassal ambition, demonstrated by %s, has temporarily revealed enemy positions.
Vassal ambition, demonstrated by %s, has revealed itself and temporarily revealed your position!
Your vassal ambition has revealed enemy positions!
The effect of 'Vassal Ambition' will end in
(Team) Arrives slowly! Provides 1 shipment for you and your allies. All enemies will be temporarily revealed on the map.
Leader of your army. Has an aura that strengthens melee infantry and carries a mercenary banner. Explores, fights, builds Town centers, Forts, and Trading Posts.

Light assault infantry that can fire at ships. Effective against heavy cavalry and artillery. Vulnerable to light infantry. Increases the durability of nearby ships and improves its own attack strength when near a ship.
Unit deals more damage near own and allied ships, and improves the hit points of nearby ships.
Heavy rifle infantry. Attacks faster as health decreases.
Fast heavy infantry, effective against cavalry.
Rifle heavy assault infantry. Effective against infantry.

Bonus: -20% <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/resource_gold.png)"> to ship build cost

Trains: Pirates, Maroons, Siege Archers

Ships: Outlaw and naval upgrades

Bonus: +1 to food trickle rate

Trains: Lisowczyks, Puskari, Organ Guns

Ships: infinite upgrades

Bonus: +0.1 <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> to land unit damage multiplier

Trains: Landwehr, Stately Grenadiers, Mounted Grenadiers

Ships: economic units

Bonus: +5 to land unit speed

Trains: Mounted Javelin Thrower, Ombilinga, Sevastopol Mortars

Ships: shipment and export upgrades
Heavy rifle infantry with bayonets for fighting cavalry. Can receive promotions.
Settlers generate additional export within the radius of their Town centers.
Trade Route upgrades become cheaper and available in any Age.
Outlaws become stronger but cost more and train slower.
Docks build ships, research technologies, and heal faster. Ships move faster and train units faster.
Destroying enemy ships and ports grants export instead of experience. Losing your own ship or port refunds 20% of their wood cost.
Ships 500 export, each technology researched at the Consulate grants additional experience.\n • Consular units gain Amber Guard status and now receive stat bonuses near Amber Guardsmen.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization Bonus:</color>
Mounted Explorer can build Stables and increases experience gained by nearby units. Advancing to new Ages allows choosing bonuses added to each shipment; each shipment also increases the training limit for Mangudai. Houses are replaced by Villages, which generate resources. Herd animals can gain weight in a Village and increase its resource production; herd animals can also be sold at a Village for wood and gold. Infantry is completely absent.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>National Guard:</color>
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"> Steppe Rider, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_cav.png)"> Keshik, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_cav.png)"> Tarkhan, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"> Mangudai, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"> Fire Thrower,
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_cav.png)"> Horse Archer, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"><icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_cav.png)"> Cataphract, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"> Iron Flail, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"> Meteor Hammer 
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique Buildings:</color>
Corral – early Stable containing Steppe Riders, Tarkhans, and Keshiks. Stable – available in a later Age, contains Horse Archers, Heavy Horse Archers, Meteor Hammer Riders, and Iron Flail Riders. Castle – combination of Outpost and Artillery Foundry, contains Hand Mortars, Fire Throwers, and Mangudai. Village – combination of Livestock Pen and Livestock Market, supports population, can create herdable horses.

Unpopular Decision (8).

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization Bonus:</color>
Influence is available as an additional resource. With each Age advancement, unique upgrades become available at the Lumber Mill. After building a Manor House, it slowly generates a free Coureur des Bois. Manor Houses mutually accelerate each other's construction speed. Explorer-settlers die without the ability to be revived, but they can be recreated; when gathering resources, they convert them into Influence. The Manor House provides health-preserving Militia in both foot and mounted variations, which can transform into other units. The Barracks offers unique unit upgrades that transform Militia into stronger and cheaper versions of those units.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>National Guard:</color>
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Métis Sharpshooter (State Militia), <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Musketeer, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Sniper, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"> Hussar,
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_cav.png)"> Dragoon, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_infantry.png)"> Militia and <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"> Mounted Militia 
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique Buildings:</color>
Mine Chapel — functions as a Consulate, periodically creating resource crates, allows forming alliances with major powers or local Native Americans and hiring their units for Influence. Lumber Mill — harvests nearby trees and generates Influence, contains upgrades unlocked with Age advancements, can plant new trees.

Unpopular Decision (4). Reworked.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization Bonus:</color>
Start the game with a Postal Wagon that can build a Watchtower or Trading Post. Advancing to new Ages has a high base cost, but each shipment and researched technology reduces advancement costs. Starting from the second Age, early revolutions to Austria and Hungary become available; their decks may differ depending on the Age from which the revolution was initiated. From Austria and Hungary, you can return to the monarchy or transition to Austria-Hungary.

<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Puskari, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Grenz Infantry, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Line Infantry, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"> Uhlan, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_cav.png)"> Reiter,
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"><icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_cav.png)"> Stutzer

<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"> Magyar Hussar, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Hungarian Grenadier, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_cav.png)"> Vlach Horse Archer, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_cav.png)"> Rosior Dragoon
</color><color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Allied Units (Austria-Hungary):</color>
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Doppelsoldner, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Prussian Skirmisher, </color>
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"><icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_cav.png)"> Mounted Grenadier
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique Buildings:</color>
Watchtower — functions as an Outpost, can once summon units for defense; the unit pool can be expanded through shipments and revolutions; shipments can also upgrade Watchtowers with bonus auras. Bank — allows automatic coin production, available in a later Age.

Unpopular Decision (10 - Anniversary).

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization Bonus:</color>
Base settler training speed is slowed. Factories are available for construction through the Town Centers or very early via shipments. Each built Factory reduces unit training speed (especially settlers) and increases building construction cost (especially houses), but speeds up advancement to the next Age. Effects from Factories persist even after their destruction and reach maximum at 10 Factories. Factories cannot train heavy artillery, and their upgrades are only available at the Capitol. The Home City deck lacks natural-economic cards and large resource crates. Stables and Outposts are unavailable, replaced by Commanderies.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>National Guard:</color>
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_light_hand_infantry.png)"> Chasseur, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Bogenschütze, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Skirmisher
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Allied Units:</color>
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"> Hussar,<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_cav.png)"> Dragoon, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Pikeman, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Halberdier, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> English Longbowman,
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"> Uhlan, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Grenadier
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique Buildings:</color>
Commandery — a defensive building that can shoot, transport units between similar structures, and train consular units; the unit pool can be expanded via Home City shipments.

Unpopular Decision (6).

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization Bonus:</color>
The Town Centers creates settlers for free and also generates gold for each building near it. The number of all buildings is limited; the main building for creating military units is the Outpost. Instead of militia and revolutions, the Town Centers has a "Mobilization" of workers, which turns settlers into musketeers but stops the creation of new settlers. Swiss Pikemen are always available in the Tavern.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>National Guard:</color>
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Crossbowman, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Pikeman, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Halberdier, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Bersagliere, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"> Hussar
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique Units:</color>
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_ranged_infantry.png)"> Alpini, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Sapper, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_cav.png)"> Mounted Crossbowman
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique Buildings:</color>
Economic Trading Company (Market) — contains settler upgrades, allows creating resource crates, ordering mines, and balancing resource quantities. Maritime Trading Company (Dock) — land equivalent of a dock, contains ship upgrades, allows ordering worker and military ships, cannot heal ships. Military Trading Company (Arsenal and Artillery Foundry) — contains infantry and cavalry upgrades, allows ordering artillery.

Unpopular Decision (3).

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization Bonus:</color>
All buildings (except walls) support population; houses are unavailable. Instead of separating military buildings into infantry and cavalry structures, buildings are divided into those for melee units and those for ranged units. When advancing to a new Age, a Balkan ally is chosen, which provides access to an upgrade shipment, as well as a new unit and a shipment containing that unit. Croats, Inquisitors, and Daredevil Cossacks are always available in the Tavern.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>National Guard:</color>
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Pikeman, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Halberdier, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"> Martolos, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Hajduk, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"> Mounted Spearman
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Allied Units:</color>
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Dorobant, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Voinik, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Klepht, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_cav.png)"> Horse Archer, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_cav.png)"> Rosior Dragoon,
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Carniolan Jäger, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Abus Gun, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"> Uskok
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique Buildings:</color>
Blockhouse - combination of an Outpost and Barracks where ranged units (including infantry and cavalry) can be trained. Barracks - partially functions as a Stable, allows training melee units (including infantry and cavalry).

Unpopular Decision (2).

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization Bonus:</color>
Starts the game with a Landmeister of the Teutonic Order, who can train commanders and build outposts. Settlers are equivalents of Coureurs des Bois, trained for wood instead of food. Settlers are much more efficient at gathering wood but have a penalty when gathering from mines. Technologies in economic buildings take longer to research. Military production buildings are available in limited numbers and produce free units after construction.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>National Guard:</color>
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Landwehr, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Schützen (Ranger), <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Musketeer, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Prussian Shooter (Skirmisher), <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"> Uhlan, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_cav.png)"> Chevau-léger, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Stately Grenadier, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"><icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_cav.png)"> Mounted Grenadier
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Teutonic Units:</color>
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Pikeman, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Crossbowman, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Halberdier, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_infantry.png)"> Anti-Skirmisher Archer 
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique Buildings:</color>
Outpost - functions similarly to a Fort, can train unique units in limited numbers and summon Teutonic units with a shared cooldown. Units garrisoned in the Outpost reduce their population cost. Native units from the Home City will be available at the Outpost.

Unpopular Decision (1). Reworked. Former Teutonic Order.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization Bonus:</color>
Influence is available as an additional resource. With each Age advancement, unique upgrades become available at the Lumber Mill. Buildings are cheap but less durable, and Houses generate Influence. Settlers are trained for wood instead of food. Explorer-settlers die without the ability to be revived, but they can be recreated; when gathering resources, they convert them into Influence. The Blockhouse and Consulate provide health-preserving Militia in both foot and mounted variations, which can transform into other units.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>National Guard:</color>
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Karelian Jäger (Skirmisher), <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_infantry.png)"> Counter-Jäger (Schiavone),
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_cav.png)"> Counter-Dragoon (Dragoon), <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Sniper, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_infantry.png)"> Militia and <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"> Mounted Militia
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique Buildings:</color>
Consulate — allows forming alliances with nearby major powers and hiring their units for Influence. Lumber Mill — harvests nearby trees and generates Influence, contains upgrades unlocked with Age advancements, can plant new trees. Blockhouse — combination of Barracks, Stable, and Outpost, contains national units. Supply Wagon — military wagon-barracks capable of transporting infantry over land and training consular units. The Dock offers unique ship upgrades, and military ships themselves are purchased for Influence.

Unpopular Decision (5). Reworked.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization Bonus:</color>
Houses are absent. The only source of population is the Town Centers, whose efficiency increases with ages and cards. Hanseatic Merchants are elite gatherers, taking 4 population slots and supporting them for themselves. When advancing, a Burgomaster is chosen, granting access to new shipments. Each advancement increases the number of Town Centers and population support. Passive experience gain is reduced, but all shipments arrive 20 seconds faster. Landsknechts and mounted bandits are always available in the tavern; Trading Posts can summon Landsknechts.

<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_villager.png)"> Hanseatic Merchant, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_ranged_infantry.png)"> Ship Guard

Uses Influence as a key resource. Economy is based on Bazaars, which convert resources, and limited slaves (Badi), who can be lost or transformed. Advancement through ages is achieved through alliances with African tribes, providing unique units and economic bonuses.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization Bonus:</color>
Start the game with Settler Wagons and a Church Wagon. Training units grants 50% experience and 50% food. The Church partially generates experience for allies. The number of Taverns is increased and can be further expanded through mercenary shipments. Taverns, after construction, create a Settler Wagon and can train them after the Fortress Age.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization Bonus:</color>
Two types of settlers are available: Sutlers improve the work of nearby Apprentices; Apprentices are available from the Town Centers. Settlers cannot construct buildings - for construction and gathering upgrades, Supply Wagons from the Town Centers are used. The unique bank Merceria produces resources of choice or creates Sutlers. All military units are available in limited quantities. The Home City offers mercenary shipments that make them available in Mercenary Camps. The Explorer-Condottiero can construct buildings for gold.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique Buildings:</color>
Merceria - produces resources or Sutlers. Villa (Hacienda) - combination of Mill, Plantation, and Livestock Pen. Mercenary Camp (Tavern) - available in greater numbers.

Unpopular Decision (9).

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization Bonus:</color>
Start the game with a garden wagon (berries for food gathering), hunting animals is not available. Houses cost more, but after construction create a wagon for the next house (reproduce in batches). All units are more expensive, train slower and are weaker. When advancing, a clan is chosen, which gives access to its shipments and allows Castles to train their units for free. Clan-bound units become passively stronger and can be further upgraded. Highlanders and mounted arquebusiers are always available in the pub. After Age V, the "Scotland Forever!" revolution becomes available.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>National Guard:</color>
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Pikeman, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Halberdier, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Musketeer, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Skirmisher, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_light_hand_infantry.png)"> Clinkern, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"> Hussar, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_cav.png)"> Dragoon, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"> Cuirassier
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique Buildings:</color>
Castle — reinforced tower, slowly produces clan unit squads for free. Grain Wagon — mobile livestock pen, creates and fattens sheep.

Unpopular Decision (11).

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization Bonus:</color>
Advancement through building Town Magistrates, which train unique settlers from different cities. Settlers work more efficiently near their home Magistrate. Amber Mines generate passive export and gold. The core army is built around the synergistic Amber Guard.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>National Guard:</color>
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Strelets, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Šautinieks, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Skirmisher, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Grenadier, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"> Hussar, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_cav.png)"> Dragoon, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_infantry.png)"> Amber Guardsman
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique Buildings:</color>
Town Magistrate (Town Centers) - Each trains unique settlers and provides specific bonuses to them. Amber Mine (Plantation) - Produces gold and export. Consulate - Hires mercenaries and technologies from European and colonial powers.

Unpopular Decision (14).
All active Town Centers create Settlers.

Cossack Settler. Not built manually, but automatically created at the Town Center. Gathers resources slower but does not require houses as it provides its own housing. Slowly regenerates health when idle.
Sends 8 random crates of 100 resources each containing food/wood/coin.
Sends 3 random crates of 400 resources each containing food/wood/coin.
Sends 5 maximally random crates of 100/500 resources each containing food/wood/coin/experience.
(Team) All military units cost less food, especially unique Cossack units.
(Team) All military units cost less gold, especially unique Cossack units.
Upon arrival of this shipment, all your current coin is exchanged for 125% food.
Upon arrival of this shipment, all your current coin is exchanged for 100% wood.
Upon arrival of this shipment, all your current food is exchanged for 100% coin.
Doubles experience gained from killing enemy units, and triples it for killing treasure guardians. Enemy units also gain double experience for killing your units.
Killing enemy units grants 30% gold of the experience gained, but killing your units also grants the enemy 10 gold.

(Team) Temporarily adds a bounty for building Settlers in the form of food, military units in the form of gold, buildings and ships in the form of wood.\n • (Delayed) With the next Hetmanate shipment – the effect is canceled.
Wall cost is replaced with 150% food, and building them now gives twice as much experience.

Provides resources for all current and future trade routes, as well as resource crates.\n • Resource crates and trade routes: future income increased by 20%\n • Trade routes: you receive 20% of all earned resources again

sends a group of Hetmans. Sends additional Hetmans for each technology researched at the Stable aimed at improving them.

Cossack elite ranged heavy infantry with colossal range. Effective against cavalry at a distance.
Cossack ranged light infantry, can use 'Cover Mode' further increasing range and defense at the cost of all speed. Effective against heavy infantry and light cavalry.
Cossack ranged light infantry, can mount a horse and become light cavalry. Effective against heavy infantry and light cavalry.
Cossack heavy infantry, can mount a horse and become heavy cavalry. Effective against cavalry and buildings.
Cossack heavy cavalry, can dismount and become heavy infantry. Effective against light infantry and artillery.
Cossack light cavalry, can dismount and become infantry. Effective against heavy cavalry and artillery.
Cossack elite heavy cavalry with a large health pool. Has very high burst damage, especially effective against light infantry and artillery.
I brought you into this world, I'll take you out!
Removes all your Settlers from the map, for each Settler removed or lost grants 100 food and 10 XP.

(Team) Allows the Hetmanate to create Cossack Daredevils at Stables. Allies gain access to creating Cossack Daredevils at Taverns, Saloons, Monasteries, and Palaces, and local nations at the Embassy. Cossack Daredevils use less population. +10 Cossack Daredevils

Sends 9 Grenadiers, grants access to their training at the Artillery Foundry, Galleon, and Fort. The specific grenadier type is chosen randomly from the following list: Pavlovsk Regiment, Hungarian Grenadiers, Turkish Humbaracı.
Instead of Settlers, 165 food is now sent from each Town Center with each shipment. Revolutions no longer require food.\n • Cancels the effect of 'Forest Region' and 'Gold Region'.
Instead of Settlers, 125 wood is now sent from each Town Center with each shipment. Revolutions no longer require wood.\n • Cancels the effect of 'Wheat Region' and 'Gold Region'.
Instead of Settlers, 150 coin is now sent from each Town Center with each shipment. Revolutions no longer require coin.\n • Cancels the effect of 'Forest Region' and 'Wheat Region'.
Sends 2 Stable Wagons and allows earlier creation of Hetmans, as well as direct training of Cossack Uhlans and Cossack Dragoons. Stables train units faster.
sends 1 Cossack Strelets, Cossack Pikeman, Serdiuk, Haidamak, and Hetman for each specific technology researched at the Blockhouse or Stable aimed at improving them.

Sends 1 Mill Wagon\n • (Delayed) With the next shipment – Mill food gathering rate: +20%

Hero Explorer – explores, fights, builds Town Centers and Factories. Fights in melee and inspires other nearby melee cavalry. Periodically uses grenades, which are very effective against treasure guardians.
Ataman's passive ability increases attack damage and speed of nearby melee cavalry by +10%.
Arrives slightly faster, sends 4 Settlers and improves food gathering from all sources.
Arrives slightly faster, sends 6 Settlers and improves coin gathering from all sources.
Arrives slightly faster, sends 10 Settlers and improves wood gathering from all sources.
(Team) Sends 4 Settlers, all natural resources provide their resource for longer.
Villagers gather food from all sources faster. Berry bushes and wild animals provide resources for longer.
Villagers earn coin from all sources faster. Mines provide resources for longer.
Unique bonus for East Indies: +1.0 resources/s and +0.25 XP/s.

Sends a group of Spanish Rodeleros. Allows re-ordering groups of Rodeleros from the Bank. \n • If a group of Rodeleros was already ordered by another shipment, only the largest group will be available from the Bank.

Here you can train and upgrade Dutch European military, including infantry and cavalry.
An East India colonist, who is trained in groups for three different resources.

Sends a Bank Wagon that can build a Bank. If you already have the maximum number of Banks, the wagon will wait in reserve.
Banks support population and their build limit is increased.

Bandits increase attack damage, hit points, cost, experience gained for their construction, and experience gained by the enemy for their kill.

Ships 2 Church Wagons increasing their build limit, reduces the cost of all technologies at the Church, and allows training Nurses in them.

Sends 1 Chateau Wagon and increases their build limit. Killing units and constructing buildings near the Chateau grants more experience.
Sends 1 Chateau Wagon and increases their build limit. The Chateau increases hit points and attack for each enemy land unit within its firing range.
Sends 1 Chateau Wagon and increases their build limit. The Chateau absorbs part of the damage dealt to nearby allies and can now be passively repaired for free.
(Team) Sends 1 Chateau Wagon and increases their build limit. Grants access to 1 random mercenary.
Sends 1 Ship of the Line. All Ships of the Line now have an aura that increases resource generation of Wineries by +5% (effect does not stack)

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization Bonus:</color>
When advancing to a new Age, a ruling dynasty is chosen, providing access to unique mercenaries at the Chateau. Settlers are trained in groups for gold, and gather resources from bushes faster. All native military units are trained one by one, but their production speed increases with each shipment sent, and their upgrades are not tied to Ages.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>National Guard:</color>
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Picard Crossbowman, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Pikeman, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Epéphancer, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Pomis, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_infantry.png)"> Franche-Comté, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_infantry.png)"> Cortaux, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"> Chevalier, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_cav.png)"> Mounted Crossbowman.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique Buildings:</color>
<icon="(18)(resources\images\icons	echs\maltese\gunpowder_mills.png)"> Winery - economic building generating food or coin by choice, and also planting berry and grape bushes.
<icon="(18)(resources\images\icons\chateau.png)"> Chateau - military-defensive building combining the functions of a Fort and a Tavern, allows hiring mercenaries and outlaws.

Unpopular Decision (17).

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization Bonus:</color>
Settlers have a penalty to gathering all resources, but slowly regenerate health and support their own population. They are created automatically from the Town centers; upon receiving a shipment, each Town center creates an additional settler. Military units become more expensive and grant more experience after upgrades. Outlaws take less population and can be further upgraded, but also become more expensive.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Shipment Mechanics:</color>
Shipments have the (Delayed) tag: they provide a small instant effect and are enhanced/weakened/cancelled after subsequent shipments. Native unit shipments send additional units for each upgrade.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>National Guard:</color>
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Cossack Rifleman, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Cossack Pikeman, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"> Cossack Uhlan, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_cav.png)"> Cossack Dragoon, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Haidamak, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Serdiuk, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"> Hetman
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique Buildings:</color>
Blockhouse — fortified building, trains units and provides defense. Village — economic building, supports population and creates livestock, can be upgraded to support more population.

Unpopular Decision (15).

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization Bonus:</color>
Settlers are trained in groups of 3 for different resources. Age advancements have different costs and times. Units are divided into native and Dutch. Banks generate gold and can send repeat shipments.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Shipment Mechanics:</color>
Unit shipments send European contingents (not available for training) and receive an infinite duplicate shipment at the Bank (requires only resources). Economic shipments and upgrades require two shipments at once and have a double effect.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Native Guard:</color>
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Javan Pikeman, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Blowpipe Warrior, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_light_hand_infantry.png)"> Kris Swordsman, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_ranged_infantry.png)"> Amok Runner with Bolas
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>European Guard:</color>
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Pikeman, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Halberdier, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)"> Skirmisher, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)"> Grenadier, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)"> Hussar, <icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_cav.png)"> Reiter
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique Buildings:</color>
Bank — generates gold, sends repeat shipments. Native Barracks — trains native units. European Barracks — trains Dutch units.

Unpopular Decision (16).

<color=0.81, 0.02, 0.12>Хартия</color> – для изучения технологии требуется <icon="(16)(resources\images\hud\resource_shipment.png)"> <color=1.0, 0.94, 0.0>поставка</color>, а по окончаю изучения <icon="(16)(resources\images\hud\resource_shipment.png)"> поставка возвращается. Из списка доступных хартий в каждом здании можно выбрать только одну, в следующей эпохе список восстановится и/или обновится.

(Team) Ships a crate of 500 XP. With each subsequent <icon="(16)(resources\images\hud\resource_shipment.png)"> <color=1.0, 0.94, 0.0>shipment</color> and/or <icon="(16)(resources\images\icons\mm_Charter.png)"><color=0.81, 0.02, 0.12> Charter</color>, 100 XP is shipped.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization Bonus:</color>
Each building has unique <icon="(16)(resources\images\icons\mm_Charter.png)"> <color=0.81, 0.02, 0.12>Charters</color> every age, which are researched by temporarily reserving shipment points (refunded after research) and provide bonuses and new age-up options. Settlers do not build houses; instead, the Parliament periodically provides builder wagons. The economy is based on Feudos, which gather two resources simultaneously. The army consists of predefined multi-unit foreign contingents, paid for with all resources.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Shipment Mechanic:</color>
The base deck size is increased. Unique shipments add effects that enhance or influence <icon="(16)(resources\images\icons\mm_Charter.png)"> <color=0.81, 0.02, 0.12>Charters</color> and <icon="(16)(resources\images\hudesource_shipment.png)"> <color=1.0, 0.94, 0.0>shipments</color>. Resource shipments invest resources into future ages, Settler shipments are replaced by resource trickles, military upgrades are presented as infinite shipments.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>National Guard:</color>
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_infantry.png)">: Pikeman, Halberdier, Janissary, Grenadier
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_skirmisher.png)">: Pavise Crossbowman, Skirmisher
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_heavy_cav.png)">: Palikar, Garrochista, Cuirassier
<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_cav.png)">: Mascati, Camel Archer, Dragoon, Arberesh (<icon="(16)(resources/images/hud/stat_icon_light_ranged_infantry.png)">)
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique Buildings:</color>
Parliament (Capitol) - produces wagons for buildings and contains key upgrades. Feudo - replaces Mills and Plantations, gathers all resources. Tax Office (replaces Tavern) - provides passive income and mercenary hiring.

Unpopular Decision (18).
Light melee cavalry. Effective against heroes, outlaws, mercenaries, as well as settlers and buildings.
Light assault infantry with muskets. Effective against all cavalry.
Light ranged cavalry. Effective against artillery and cavalry, especially heavy cavalry.
Allows Hetmans to deal melee damage that ignores armor (<icon="(16)(resources\images\hud\stat_icon_siege.png)"> Siege).
Allows Cossacks to deal melee damage that ignores armor (<icon="(16)(resources\images\hud\stat_icon_siege.png)"> Siege).

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization Bonus:</color>
The economy is based on Veerhuysen - multifunctional farms that provide population, resource gathering, livestock, and military production. Livestock is harder to steal. Age advancement is possible for various resources or shipments. All upgrades and shipments from the Metropolis arrive through a shared queue. All buildings are constructed via the universal Trekboer Wagon.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique Units:</color>
Rykburger, Schepens, Khoisan Hunter, Voortrekker, Cape Ranger, Bergenaid.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique Buildings:</color>
Veerhuysen - replaces the House, Mill, and partially the Barracks. Trekboer Wagon - a universal construction wagon capable of building any structure.
